goal: CFA Level I Exam - Core Content Mastery
description: Complete the curriculum and practice questions for the CFA Level I exam, focusing on high-weight areas like Ethics and Financial Statement Analysis.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Working full-time 40 hours per week; study limited to evenings and weekends.
baseline.physical_constraints: Suffer from eye fatigue; requires Blue Light filters and 20-20-20 rule breaks.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must achieve >70% in all Ethics practice modules before taking the final mock.

tasks.description: Sign up for the exam and install the CFA Institute candidate app and curriculum files.
Read the Standards of Professional Conduct and complete the 200 associated practice questions.
Master Time Value of Money (TVM) and probability distributions using the financial calculator.
Review Income Statements, Balance Sheets, and Cash Flow analysis under IFRS and US GAAP.
Study bond valuation, duration, and equity valuation models (DDM, Multipliers).
Take the first official CFA Institute mock exam under timed, proctored-style conditions.

goal: Daily Professional Portfolio Maintenance
description: A daily habit of documenting technical work or learning to maintain an up-to-date public-facing portfolio (GitHub/Personal Site).

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be done after the work day ends at 5:30 PM.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: No sensitive employer-owned code may be uploaded.

tasks.description: Identify one code snippet, diagram, or paragraph written today that represents a lesson learned.
Remove any private API keys, company names, or internal proprietary logic from the artifact.
Add the artifact to a public 'Today-I-Learned' or 'Project-Scratchpad' repo with a clear README note.

goal: Obtain Real Estate Salesperson License
description: Complete the 75-hour pre-license course, pass the course final, and successfully clear the state licensing examination.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Course must be completed within a 90-day window from enrollment.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires stable internet for proctored online exams.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must achieve a minimum score of 75% on all practice quizzes before the state exam.

tasks.task: Complete Property Ownership and Land Use modules
tasks.description: Watch all video lectures and read the text for Chapters 1-4 of the pre-license curriculum.
Draft a visual guide explaining Metes and Bounds, Lot and Block, and Government Survey systems.

tasks.task: Complete Valuation and Market Analysis modules
tasks.description: Review the Sales Comparison, Cost, and Income approaches to appraisal.
Solve 20 problems involving commission splits, proration, and loan-to-value ratios.
Complete the 100-question proctored course final exam to receive the certificate of completion.
Submit the course certificate to the state board and book the earliest available testing slot at a PSI center.

goal: Daily SAT Reading Comprehension Practice
description: Build stamina and accuracy for the Evidence-Based Reading section of the SAT through consistent daily passage analysis.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be done at a desk using a physical pencil for marking.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must set a timer for every passage to track speed.

tasks.description: Read one SAT-style passage (History or Science) and underline the main claim and three pieces of supporting evidence.
Answer the 10-11 questions associated with the passage under timed conditions.
Check answers and for every mistake, identify if it was a 'Evidence' error, 'Vocabulary in Context' error, or 'Main Idea' error.

goal: Pass the NCLEX-RN Examination
description: Comprehensive review of nursing principles, pharmacology, and clinical judgment to obtain a Registered Nurse license.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must study around a part-time clinical rotation schedule.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires a quiet space to practice 'Next Generation NCLEX' (NGN) case studies.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must complete the entire Kaplan or UWorld question bank.

tasks.description: Study major drug classes, side effects, and nursing considerations for cardiovascular and endocrine medications.

tasks.task: Safe and Effective Care: Management of Care
tasks.description: Review principles of delegation, prioritization, and informed consent.
Work through 5 'Next Generation' unfolding case studies focusing on clinical judgment.
Review therapeutic communication techniques and developmental stages (Erikson/Piaget).
Take a Computerized Adaptive Testing (CAT) mock exam that shuts off between 75 and 145 questions.

goal: Daily 20-minute 'Mental Priming' for Study
description: Establish a consistent pre-study routine to reduce cognitive friction and transition into a state of deep focus.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be performed immediately before the first study session of the day.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: No digital devices or music allowed during the priming sequence.

tasks.description: Physically clear the desk of all items not related to the current subject.
Perform 2 minutes of box breathing (4s in, 4s hold, 4s out, 4s hold) to lower heart rate.
Handwrite one specific goal for the upcoming study block and the primary benefit of achieving it.
Read the table of contents or summary of the previous session to activate relevant schemas.

goal: Master the 'Polya Method' for Mathematical Problem Solving
description: Internalize George Polya's 4-step problem-solving framework (Understand, Plan, Execute, Look Back) to tackle difficult non-standard math problems.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must apply the method to at least 3 problems per session.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must write out the 'Plan' step explicitly in English before writing any equations.

tasks.description: Read the summary of Polya's 'How to Solve It' and memorize the four phases: Understand the Problem, Devise a Plan, Carry out the Plan, Look Back.
Take 5 complex word problems and ONLY write down the 'Unknown', the 'Data', and the 'Condition'. Do not solve them.
For the same 5 problems, write down the strategy name (e.g., 'Draw a Figure', 'Work Backwards', 'Solve a Simpler Problem') to be used.
Solve 3 'easy' difficulty competition problems using the full written framework.
Take a previously solved problem and find a second, different method to derive the solution.

goal: Daily Chess Tactics for Pattern Recognition
description: Improve calculation speed and tactical awareness by solving specific chess puzzles daily.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use a 'real board' mode or hide the solution button.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must calculate the full variation to checkmate/material gain before moving the first piece.

tasks.description: Solve 5 simple 'Mate in 1' puzzles as fast as possible to prime the brain.
Open one 'Hard' difficulty puzzle. Spend up to 10 minutes calculating the tree of variations without moving pieces.
Review the 'Hard' puzzle with the computer engine to see if any defensive resources were missed.
Identify the tactical motif used in the puzzle (e.g., 'Pin', 'Skewer', 'Removing the Defender') and write it down.

goal: Resolve persistent high-latency issue in web application using Five Whys
description: Systematically identify and fix the root cause of 500ms+ database query delays during peak traffic hours.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use existing server resources; no budget for hardware upgrades.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: The fix must be tested in a staging environment before production deployment.

tasks.description: Query the last 24 hours of logs to isolate specific API endpoints with latency exceeding 500ms.
For the slowest endpoint, recursively ask 'Why?' until the underlying infrastructure or logic flaw is identified.
Write a specific technical solution based on the root cause identified (e.g., adding a composite index or refactoring a join).
Apply the fix to the staging database and run a load test to simulate peak traffic.
Merge the fix to the production branch and monitor the latency dashboard for 1 hour.

goal: Daily 'Rubber Duck' code review habit
description: Perform a daily systematic verbal walkthrough of new code to identify logic flaws and edge cases before they become bugs.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be done at the start of the final hour of the workday.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires a quiet space where talking out loud is permitted.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must physically explain the code to an inanimate object or teammate.

tasks.description: Choose a section of code written today that contains the most complex logic or conditional branching.
Explain out loud exactly what each line of code is doing and what the expected inputs/outputs are.
Identify three potential inputs that could cause the code to fail (e.g., null values, empty strings, max integers).

goal: Diagnose and repair a recurring basement drainage issue
description: Systematically identify the cause of water pooling in the basement during heavy rain and implement a drainage solution.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Limited to hand tools and a basic wet-dry vacuum; no heavy excavation machinery.
baseline.physical_constraints: Must work in a low-clearance crawl space.
baseline.non_negotiables: No modifications allowed to the shared foundation wall with the neighbor.

tasks.description: Using a garden hose, spray water on the exterior grading and foundation to pinpoint exactly where water is infiltrating.
Check all gutters for debris and ensure downspout extensions are directing water at least 6 feet away from the foundation.
Use a plumbing snake to clear any sediment or blockages from the main basement floor drain.
Clean the area around the identified crack and pack it with hydraulic cement until flush with the wall.
Add 5 bags of topsoil to the exterior area near the leak, sloping the soil away from the foundation.
Re-simulate heavy rain with the garden hose for 15 minutes over the repaired area.

goal: Daily first-principles thinking exercise
description: Deconstruct one complex societal or personal problem every day into its fundamental truths to develop original solutions.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be performed in a physical notebook to avoid digital distraction.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must identify at least three 'assumptions' that are usually taken for granted.

tasks.description: Identify a complex problem you usually accept at face value (e.g., 'Why is housing expensive?' or 'Why can't I wake up early?').
List all the things people 'know' to be true about this problem. Circle the ones that are not fundamental physical laws.
Break the problem down to its 'atoms'—the undeniable truths (e.g., 'A house is made of wood, labor, and land').
Design a new solution based *only* on the fundamental truths, ignoring the previous assumptions.

goal: Master the Fundamentals of Dynamic Programming (DP)
description: Transition from brute-force recursive thinking to optimized memoization and tabulation techniques for algorithmic problems.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Python or Java for implementations.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must verify time and space complexity for every solution derived.

tasks.description: Take the 'Fibonacci Sequence' and 'Climbing Stairs' problems and draw out the full recursive decision tree on paper.
Solve 'House Robber' using a recursive function and a hash map/array to cache results.
Refactor the 'House Robber' solution to use an iterative loop and a DP table, removing recursion.
Further optimize the Tabulation solution to reduce space complexity from O(n) to O(1) using variables.
Apply the framework to 'Unique Paths' or 'Minimum Path Sum'. define the state transition equation: dp[i][j] = ...
Study the 0/1 Knapsack problem and implementing the 2D array solution.

goal: Daily Manual Code Tracing (Dry Run)
description: Develop the ability to simulate code execution mentally or on paper to improve debugging skills and logic verification.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use pen and paper; no computer execution allowed until the end.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must explicitly track the values of all variables at every iteration.

tasks.description: Find a code snippet (10-20 lines) involving nested loops, recursion, or pointer manipulation.
Draw a table with columns for the Line Number, Variable Names, and Output/Console.
Manually 'run' the code line by line, updating the variable values in the table for a specific input set.
Run the code on a computer with the same input and compare the actual final state with the trace table.

goal: Build a Visual Pathfinding Tool
description: Create a web-based application that visualizes how Dijkstra’s and A* algorithms find the shortest path on a user-defined grid with obstacles.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Vanilla JavaScript and HTML5 Canvas (no external game engines).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: The visualization must allow the user to place walls in real-time.

tasks.description: Render a 2D grid of 'nodes' on a canvas and implement a coordinate system to track start and end points.
Write a function to transform the grid into an Adjacency List representation for the pathfinding logic.
Implement the core Dijkstra’s logic using a priority queue to find the shortest path from start to end.
Modify the algorithm to trigger a 'draw' function for every node visited, creating an animation of the expansion.
Implement the Manhattan Distance heuristic to upgrade the search to A* and compare it with Dijkstra’s.

goal: Daily Algorithm Benchmarking
description: Establish a habit of analyzing and timing different algorithmic approaches to the same problem to develop an intuition for efficiency.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use the system time library for nanosecond precision.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Each session must compare at least two different time complexities (e.g., O(n²) vs O(n log n)).

tasks.description: Solve a single problem using a naive approach and an optimized approach (e.g., Bubble Sort vs Merge Sort).
Run both functions against a large input dataset (n=10,000+) and record the execution time in milliseconds.
Plot the results or calculate the growth rate to confirm that the empirical results match the theoretical Big O complexity.

goal: Design a Scalable URL Shortener Service (System Design)
description: Create a high-level design document for a service like TinyURL that can handle 100 million new URLs per month.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must identify specific database choices (SQL vs NoSQL).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include 'Back-of-the-Envelope' calculations for storage and bandwidth.

tasks.description: Calculate read/write QPS and total storage needed for 5 years based on 100M monthly writes and a 10:1 read/write ratio.
Define the REST API endpoints (e.g., createShortURL, getLongURL) including input parameters and HTTP return codes.
Design the data model to store the mapping (ShortURL, OriginalURL, CreationDate, Expiration) and justify the choice of database.
Choose a strategy for generating unique short keys (e.g., Base62 encoding of a counter vs. MD5 hash) and handle collisions.
Draw the full system including Load Balancers, Web Servers, Database Shards, and Caching layers.

goal: Implement a Thread-Safe Bounded Blocking Queue
description: Write a custom implementation of a blocking queue (Producer-Consumer pattern) using low-level synchronization primitives to master concurrency.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use a language with explicit threading support (Java, C++, Go).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use Condition Variables (or wait/notify) rather than high-level library queues.

tasks.description: Create the class structure with `enqueue` and `dequeue` methods and an underlying circular buffer or linked list.
Add a Mutex (or ReentrantLock) to protect the shared state during enqueue and dequeue operations.
Add logic to `wait()` when the queue is full (producer) or empty (consumer) and `signal()` when state changes.
Write a test script that spawns 5 producer threads and 5 consumer threads to stress-test the queue.

goal: Daily Engineering Blog Analysis
description: Read and deconstruct one engineering blog post (e.g., Uber, Netflix, Meta) daily to learn real-world architecture patterns.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must extract the 'Problem', 'Solution', and 'Trade-off' from each article.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must draw a rough sketch of the architecture described.

tasks.description: Choose a high-quality technical article focusing on scaling, database migration, or microservices.
Identify the specific constraint the team faced and what they sacrificed to solve it (e.g., Consistency for Availability).
Draw the system diagram on a notepad based on the text description.

goal: Master the 'Sliding Window' Algorithmic Pattern
description: Deep dive into the Sliding Window technique to intuitively solve substring and subarray problems without brute force.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must implement solutions in Python.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must write a reusable code template for 'Variable Size' window problems.

tasks.description: Draw the difference between a Fixed Window (e.g., max sum of size k) and Variable Window (e.g., smallest subarray with sum >= s).
Solve 'Maximum Average Subarray I' and 'Find All Anagrams in a String'.
Solve 'Minimum Size Subarray Sum'. Focus on the `while` loop condition for shrinking the window.
Solve 'Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters'.
Write a generic code skeleton that handles window expansion, constraint checking, and window shrinking.

goal: Weekly Virtual Contest Simulation
description: Simulate the pressure of a real coding interview or LeetCode Weekly Contest to build speed and stress resilience.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be done on Saturday or Sunday mornings.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Zero access to Google, StackOverflow, or previous submissions during the 90 minutes.

tasks.description: Open the Virtual Contest page, put phone in 'Do Not Disturb', and start the 90-minute timer.
Attempt to solve the 4 contest problems (Easy, Medium, Medium, Hard) strictly within the time limit.
Check the results to see which test cases failed and record the ranking/score.
For one problem that was NOT solved, read the editorial and implement the solution without looking at the text.

goal: Daily Pattern Recognition Drill
description: Train the brain to instantly identify the correct data structure or algorithm for a problem without actually writing the code.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must cover at least 5 different problem types.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Do not write code; this is purely a classification exercise.

tasks.description: Open a random LeetCode problem and read the description and constraints (e.g., n < 10^5).
Within 60 seconds, verbally state the pattern (e.g., 'This is Top K Elements using a Heap' or 'This is Graph BFS').
Reveal the 'Related Topics' tags or check the discussion titles to verify if the hypothesis was correct.
Briefly note *why* the constraints pointed to that solution (e.g., 'n=20 suggests O(2^n) recursion').

goal: Master Backtracking Algorithms
description: Understand and implement backtracking to solve combinatorial problems like N-Queens, Sudoku, and Permutations.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must visualize the recursion tree for at least 3 problems.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement state pruning (optimizations) where applicable.

tasks.description: Draw the full state space tree for 'Permutations of [1, 2, 3]' on paper.
Solve 'Subsets' (Power Set) using the 'include vs exclude' decision pattern.
Solve 'N-Queens'. Implement the `isValid` function to check diagonals and columns efficiently.
Solve 'Sudoku Solver' but optimize by trying the cell with the fewest options first (Most Constrained Variable).

goal: System Design: Distributed Rate Limiter
description: Design a system to limit API requests (e.g., 100 req/min) using Redis and sliding window logs.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must handle race conditions in a distributed environment.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must compare Fixed Window vs Sliding Window Log vs Token Bucket algorithms.

tasks.description: Create a comparison matrix of Token Bucket, Leaky Bucket, and Sliding Window Counters.
Design the data structure (e.g., Sorted Sets) to store timestamps for the 'Sliding Window Log' approach.
Draw the flow: Client -> Middleware (Rate Limiter) -> Redis -> Backend Service.

goal: Graph Theory: Topological Sort Mastery
description: Master dependency resolution problems (e.g., Course Schedule, Build Systems) using Kahn's Algorithm and DFS.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must implement both BFS (Kahn's) and DFS approaches.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must correctly detect cycles in a directed graph.

tasks.description: Define 'Indegree' and draw a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) with its indegree table.
Solve 'Course Schedule II' using a Queue and Indegree array.
Implement the 'visited' and 'recursion stack' (path) arrays to detect cycles in a DFS traversal.

goal: Optimize Database Performance with Indexing
description: Analyze slow SQL queries and implement specific indexing strategies (B-Tree, Hash, Composite) to reduce execution time.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` (Postgres) or equivalent to verify results.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must demonstrate the difference between a Full Table Scan and an Index Scan.

tasks.description: Populate a table with 1 million rows of random user data.
Run a `SELECT * FROM users WHERE last_name = 'Smith'` query and record the execution time and plan.
Create a B-Tree index on `last_name` and re-run the query.
Test a query filtering by `last_name` AND `age`. Compare separate indexes vs a single composite index.

goal: Implement a Trie (Prefix Tree) for Auto-Complete
description: Build a Trie data structure from scratch to handle string insertion and prefix searching efficiently.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must define a custom `TrieNode` class.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Search complexity must be O(L) where L is the word length.

tasks.description: Define the `TrieNode` with a HashMap (or array size 26) of children and a boolean `isEndOfWord`.
Implement the method to add a word by iterating through chars and creating nodes if they don't exist.
Implement `startsWith(prefix)` to return true if any word in the Trie begins with the given string.

goal: Daily Bit Manipulation Tricks
description: Learn one bitwise operation trick per day to optimize code (e.g., checking parity, swapping without temp, isolating bits).

baseline.fixed_constraints: No calculator allowed; verify binary math on paper.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must apply the trick to a coding problem.

tasks.description: Study one specific operator (e.g., XOR `^`, Left Shift `<<`) and its common use case (e.g., `x & (x-1)` clears the lowest set bit).
Solve one 'Bit Manipulation' tagged problem on LeetCode (e.g., 'Number of 1 Bits', 'Single Number').

goal: Heap / Priority Queue Deep Dive
description: Master the usage of Min-Heaps and Max-Heaps for 'Top K' problems and median maintenance.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must implement a custom Heap class (push/pop/heapify) at least once.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must distinguish when to use a Heap vs Sorting.

tasks.description: Write a Min-Heap using an array implementation, handling the `bubbleUp` and `sinkDown` logic.
Solve 'Top K Frequent Elements' using a Min-Heap of size K to keep track of the largest elements seen so far.
Solve 'Find Median from Data Stream' using two heaps (one Min, one Max).

goal: Design Patterns: Singleton & Factory
description: Implement two fundamental Creational Design Patterns to manage object instantiation.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be thread-safe implementations.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must explain *why* these patterns are used (e.g., decoupling, resource control).

tasks.description: Write a Thread-Safe Singleton (e.g., using 'Double-Checked Locking' in Java or `sync.Once` in Go).
Create a `ShapeFactory` that returns objects of type `Circle`, `Square`, or `Rectangle` based on string input.

goal: Master Binary Search on Answer Space
description: Learn to apply Binary Search not just to arrays, but to solution ranges (e.g., 'Minimize the Maximum' problems).

baseline.fixed_constraints: None.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must define the monotonic condition (predicate function) clearly.

tasks.description: Recognize the pattern: If checking `x` is valid implies `x+1` is valid (or vice versa), binary search applies.
For 'Koko Eating Bananas', write the helper function `canEatAll(speed)` that returns true/false.
Implement the binary search loop (left, right, mid) calling the predicate function.

goal: Weekly Code Refactoring Marathon
description: Revisit old solutions or personal projects to improve readability, modularity, and variable naming.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be done on Sunday evenings.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Code must not break existing functionality (regression testing).

tasks.description: Open a project or LeetCode submission from >1 month ago.
Identify 'Magic Numbers', massive functions, or unclear variable names (e.g., `temp`, `x`, `arr`).
Extract helper functions, rename variables to be descriptive, and add comments where logic is complex.

goal: Master Monotonic Stack and Queue Patterns
description: Learn to solve 'Next Greater Element' and 'Sliding Window Maximum' problems using monotonic data structures to achieve O(n) performance.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Python or C++ for implementation.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to explain the logic for why elements are popped (maintaining the 'monotonic' property).

tasks.description: Trace the stack state for the array [2, 1, 2, 4, 3] to find the 'Next Greater Element'.
Solve 'Next Greater Element I' on LeetCode using a decreasing stack and a hash map.
Use a Deque to maintain a monotonic decreasing window to solve 'Sliding Window Maximum'.

goal: Fast & Slow Pointers (Tortoise and Hare)
description: Master the two-pointer technique for detecting cycles and finding midpoints in linear data structures.

baseline.fixed_constraints: None.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must derive the mathematical proof for why the pointers meet in a cycle.

tasks.description: Solve 'Linked List Cycle'. Implement the logic where `fast` moves twice as fast as `slow`.
Solve 'Linked List Cycle II'. Prove and implement the logic to find the start of the loop.

goal: Union-Find (Disjoint Set Union) Mastery
description: Learn to manage disjoint sets and detect connectivity in graphs with nearly constant time complexity.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must implement Path Compression and Union by Rank/Size.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to explain why the time complexity is the Inverse Ackermann function.

tasks.description: Write a `UnionFind` class with `find` and `union` methods.
Solve 'Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph'.
Solve 'Redundant Connection' by finding the edge that creates a cycle in a tree.

goal: Master Greedy Algorithms (Intervals & Optimization)
description: Learn to identify when a locally optimal choice leads to a globally optimal solution, focusing on Interval Scheduling and Jump Game problems.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must prove the greedy choice property logic (even informally) before coding.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must sort intervals correctly based on end times or start times depending on the problem.

tasks.description: Solve 'Non-overlapping Intervals'. Sort by *end time* to maximize the number of non-overlapping intervals.
Solve 'Merge Intervals'. Sort by *start time* and merge overlapping ranges linearly.
Solve 'Jump Game' using a greedy `max_reachable` index. Avoid Dynamic Programming (O(n^2)) in favor of O(n) greedy.

goal: Advanced Tree Traversals & Serialization
description: Move beyond simple recursion to handle Tree Serialization and O(1) Space Traversals (Morris Traversal).

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must implement iterative solutions using a Stack.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must handle null values correctly during serialization.

tasks.description: Implement a codec to convert a tree to a string (e.g., '1,2,X,X,3,X,X') and back using Preorder traversal.
Implement Inorder traversal without recursion or a stack by creating temporary links to predecessors (Threading).
Solve 'Vertical Order Traversal of a Binary Tree' using BFS and a sorting strategy for column/row indices.

goal: Segment Tree Implementation (Range Queries)
description: Build a Segment Tree to handle Range Sum Queries with updates in logarithmic time, overcoming the limitations of Prefix Sum arrays.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must implement array-based tree representation (index `2*i` and `2*i+1`).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must achieve O(log n) for both update and query operations.

tasks.description: Write the `buildTree` function to recursively divide the array range and store sums in the tree array.
Implement the `update(index, val)` function to modify a leaf node and recalculate sums up to the root.
Implement `query(left, right)` to return the sum of a specific range by combining relevant tree nodes.

goal: Master LRU Cache Design & Implementation
description: Implement a Least Recently Used (LRU) Cache from scratch using a Doubly Linked List and a Hash Map to achieve O(1) performance for both get and put operations.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must not use built-in OrderedDict or LinkedHashMap libraries.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: The cache must maintain a fixed capacity and evict the least recently used item when full.

tasks.description: Define the `Node` class for a doubly linked list (key, value, prev, next) and initialize the `LRUCache` class with dummy head and tail nodes.
Implement `add_node` (to head) and `remove_node` logic to handle the pointer updates for the doubly linked list.
Implement the `get` method to move accessed items to the head and the `put` method to insert new items or update existing ones, handling eviction if capacity is reached.

goal: Network Flow: Max Flow Min Cut
description: Master the Ford-Fulkerson method and Edmonds-Karp algorithm to solve maximum flow problems in flow networks.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must implement the algorithm using an adjacency matrix or list to represent capacities.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to identify the minimum cut from the saturated edges after finding the max flow.

tasks.description: Draw a small 4-node network and manually construct its residual graph after a single augmenting path is found.
Implement the Ford-Fulkerson method using BFS to find the shortest augmenting path (Edmonds-Karp).
Run a final BFS/DFS on the residual graph to find all nodes reachable from the source; the edges leaving this set constitute the Min-Cut.

goal: Daily Algorithm Debugging Challenge
description: Identify and fix logical bugs in provided 'broken' code snippets to sharpen analytical skills and knowledge of language-specific pitfalls.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be done without using an IDE debugger (manual tracing only).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must provide a fix that passes original test cases.

tasks.description: Read a snippet containing common errors (e.g., off-by-one, integer overflow, improper scoping) and find the flaw.
Rewrite the function or modify the specific lines to resolve the issue.

goal: Master Bitmask Dynamic Programming
description: Learn to solve optimization problems involving small sets (n < 20) by using bitmasks to represent states in Dynamic Programming.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must implement in a language that supports bitwise operators efficiently (Python/C++).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to calculate the total number of states ($$2^n \times n$$) before implementation.

tasks.description: Practice checking if the i-th bit is set, setting the i-th bit, and toggling bits using `&`, `|`, and `^`.
Solve the TSP using DP with Bitmask to find the shortest possible route that visits every city exactly once.
Solve a problem where N tasks are assigned to N people with varying costs, minimizing the total cost.

goal: Balanced Search Trees: Red-Black Tree Rotations
description: Master the self-balancing logic of Red-Black trees, focusing on left/right rotations and color flipping.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must maintain the 5 core Red-Black properties during all operations.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must manually trace a sequence of 10 insertions on paper before coding.

tasks.description: Draw a 3-node unbalanced tree and show the step-by-step pointer updates for a Left Rotation.
Implement the `insertFixUp` method that handles the 3 cases: Uncle is Red, Uncle is Black (Line), and Uncle is Black (Triangle).

goal: Daily Algorithm Optimization Log
description: A daily habit of taking a working $O(n^2)$ solution and refactoring it to $O(n \log n)$ or $O(n)$ using advanced data structures.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use a profiler or simple timer to prove the performance gain.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must write a one-sentence explanation of the 'bottleneck' in the original version.

tasks.description: Analyze a slow solution and identify the redundant operation (e.g., nested loop searching for a value).
Replace the slow part with a Hash Map, Heap, or Binary Search to reduce the time complexity.

goal: Master B-Tree Data Structures for Database Engine Design
description: Understand the multi-way search tree logic used in production database indexing (like MySQL and PostgreSQL) to manage disk-resident data.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must focus on the 'Degree' (m) of the tree and its effect on height.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must manually trace the 'Node Splitting' and 'Borrowing/Merging' logic for deletions.

tasks.description: Draw a B-Tree of degree 3 and explain why every node (except root) must have at least $\lceil m/2 \rceil$ children.
Trace the insertion of 10 keys that force at least two node splits. Document the 'median promotion' step.

goal: Implement a Suffix Trie for Fast Pattern Matching
description: Build a data structure that allows for $O(m)$ substring searches where $m$ is the pattern length, regardless of the text size.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must handle the termination character (e.g., '$') correctly.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to find all occurrences of a pattern in a large text.

tasks.description: Given a string like 'BANANA$', list all suffixes and insert them into a standard Trie.
Implement a search function that traverses the Trie based on a query string.

goal: Daily 'Visual Memory' Data Structure Drill
description: A daily 15-minute habit of sketching a complex data structure (e.g., AVL Tree, Skip List) from memory to solidify architectural intuition.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use physical paper and pencil; no undo button.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must label all pointers (next, prev, parent, child) and state variables (height, color, balance factor).

tasks.description: Use a randomizer to pick one data structure from a curated list of 20.
Draw the structure containing at least 7 nodes, ensuring all internal properties are respected.
Compare the sketch to a textbook reference and circle any errors (e.g., a missing null pointer or incorrect balance factor).

goal: Implement a Skip List Data Structure
description: Build a probabilistic alternative to balanced trees that supports search, insertion, and deletion in O(log n) time.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must implement in Java or C++ to handle manual pointer references.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include a visual print method to verify the layered structure.

tasks.description: Define the `SkipNode` class containing the value and an array (or list) of 'forward' pointers for different levels.
Implement the coin-flip logic to determine the height of a new node (e.g., 50% chance to promote to the next level).
Write the `search(value)` method that starts at the top level and moves down/right to find the target.
Implement `insert(value)`: Find the update path, create the new node, and re-link pointers at every level up to the node's height.
Implement `delete(value)`: Locate the node and update predecessor pointers to skip over it at all levels.
Create a `printList()` method that displays the list level-by-level to verify the tower structure.

goal: Master Fenwick Tree (Binary Indexed Tree) Data Structure
description: Learn to implement and apply Binary Indexed Trees (BIT) to solve range query problems with O(log n) updates, replacing slower Segment Trees for specific use cases.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must rely on bitwise operations (LSB extraction) rather than explicit tree pointers.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must verify the 1-based indexing requirement common in BIT implementations.

tasks.description: Write a short comparison between Prefix Sum Arrays (O(1) query, O(n) update) and Fenwick Trees (O(log n) for both).
Practice the isolation logic `x & (-x)` by manually calculating the 'parent' index for 5 random integers on paper.
Implement `update(index, delta)` to add a value to an element and propagate the change up to relevant 'ancestors' using the LSB addition logic.
Implement `query(index)` to traverse down from the given index to 0 by subtracting the LSB to accumulate the sum.
Implement `range_query(left, right)` using the formula `query(right) - query(left - 1)`.
Solve the 'Count Inversions' problem (finding how many pairs (i, j) exist where i < j and A[i] > A[j]) using a BIT to track frequencies.

goal: Establish a Daily Algorithmic Pattern Recognition Habit
description: Develop a consistent routine for identifying and implementing fundamental data structure patterns to maintain technical interview readiness.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Study sessions must occur between 7:00 AM and 8:30 AM to avoid workday fatigue.
baseline.physical_constraints: Must use a laptop with a blue light filter to prevent eye strain.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must write at least one line of code per session; reading only is not permitted.

tasks.description: Select one specific algorithmic pattern (e.g., Sliding Window, Two Pointers, or BFS) from a curated list.
Identify three key constraints in the problem (e.g., time complexity requirements or input size) that justify the chosen pattern.
Draft the logical flow of the solution using comments, focusing on edge cases like empty inputs or single-element sets.
Translate the pseudo-code into working code and run it against the provided test cases.
Review the runtime and memory usage; identify one specific way to reduce space or time overhead.

goal: Build a Deep Learning Library from Scratch
description: Implement a minimal autograd engine and neural network library in Python using only NumPy to understand backpropagation and tensor operations.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Cannot use PyTorch, TensorFlow, or JAX.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must successfully train a multi-layer perceptron to solve the MNIST digit classification problem.

tasks.description: Implement a Value class that stores a scalar and its gradient, supporting basic arithmetic (+, *) and building a computational graph for backpropagation.
Generalize the scalar engine to handle multi-dimensional NumPy arrays (Tensors) and implement vectorized addition and matrix multiplication.
Implement ReLU, Sigmoid, and Softmax activation functions along with Cross-Entropy loss including their local gradients.
Build a 'Module' base class, a 'Linear' layer, and an SGD optimizer that updates weights using the stored gradients.
Create a data loader for MNIST, initialize a 2-layer network, and run a training loop for 5 epochs.

goal: Daily GRE Quant Practice Routine
description: Maintain a consistent high-accuracy habit for GRE quantitative reasoning focusing on geometry, algebra, and data interpretation.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use the official GRE on-screen calculator emulator.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must not spend more than 1 minute and 45 seconds on any single question.

tasks.description: Solve 10 random Quant problems from the Manhattan Prep or Official ETS pool.
Identify 3 problems where 'D' (cannot be determined) is the correct answer and explain the logic.
Document any incorrect answers in a master spreadsheet, categorizing by concept (e.g., 'Prime Numbers' or 'Triangles').
Review 20 flashcards for common fraction-to-decimal conversions and squares up to 25.

goal: Implement a Relational Database Engine (Mini-SQL)
description: Develop a simple disk-based storage engine that supports basic SQL operations, indexing, and persistent storage.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use C or Rust for memory safety and manual pointer management.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Data must persist on disk after the program exits.

tasks.description: Implement a Pager that reads/writes fixed-size pages (4KB) from a binary file to memory.
Define the binary layout of a leaf node, including headers (type, parent, key count) and cells (key, value).
Implement a schema definition and a compact binary format to store user rows (ID, Username, Email).
Create a cursor that can iterate through all rows in the B-Tree leaf nodes from start to finish.
Implement a simple Command Line Interface that parses 'insert' and 'select' statements using string splitting.

goal: Establish a Daily Morning 'Code Kata' Habit
description: Solve one small, logic-focused coding problem every morning to improve syntax fluency and edge-case thinking.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be done between 7:30 AM and 8:00 AM.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must not use AI assistants or search for the solution until the task is complete.

tasks.description: Choose a 6 kyu or 7 kyu level problem from CodeWars or a 'Simple' tagged problem on LeetCode.
Write 3 test cases, including one 'happy path' and two edge cases (e.g., empty string, negative number).
Write the function to pass all test cases.

goal: Prepare for Professional Cloud Architect Certification
description: Pass the Google Cloud Professional Cloud Architect exam by mastering resource management, networking, and high availability design.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Exam date is set for day 31.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must pass two full-length practice exams with >80% before sitting for the real exam.

tasks.description: Review differences between GCE, GKE, App Engine, and Cloud Functions. Study Cloud Spanner vs Cloud SQL use cases.
Study VPC Peering, Shared VPCs, Cloud Interconnect, and Hybrid Connectivity options.
Read the three official GCP exam case studies (e.g., Mountkirk Games) and map solutions to their business requirements.
Take a 50-question timed practice exam in a silent room.
Review every missed question from Practice Exam A and watch deep-dive videos on those specific topics.

goal: Establish a consistent study routine for the CPA (Certified Public Accountant) FAR section exam
description: A sustainable daily and weekly routine to cover the Financial Accounting and Reporting syllabus without burnout, focusing on active recall and consistent practice.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Full-time job (9am-6pm), commute time is 45 mins each way (public transit).
baseline.physical_constraints: Mental fatigue high on weekdays after 7pm.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must sleep by 11pm. No studying on Friday evenings.

tasks.description: Review vocabulary and core accounting formulas using spaced repetition software during the morning commute.
Read one sub-chapter or watch one lecture video on a new concept during the lunch break.
Complete a set of Multiple Choice Questions related to the day's concept to test immediate understanding.
Document incorrect answers from the MCQ session into the error tracking spreadsheet with a brief explanation of why the answer was wrong.
Complete complex Task-Based Simulations (TBS) that require synthesizing multiple concepts.

goal: Submit Final CS Capstone Project and Thesis
description: Complete the documentation, code cleanup, and final submission for the 'AI Traffic Control' senior project.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Classes on M/W/F until 2 PM. Part-time work T/Th 1 PM - 5 PM.
baseline.physical_constraints: Cannot type for more than 45 minutes without a stretch break (RSI risk).
baseline.non_negotiables: No coding after 10 PM to ensure sleep quality.

tasks.description: Collect all 15 cited papers and format them into the BibTeX file.
Write the 1,500-word chapter detailing the algorithm selection and data preprocessing steps.
Clean up the main event loop in `server.py` to remove commented-out debug code and add proper logging.
Run the auto-doc tool to generate HTML documentation for the endpoints.
Record a 5-minute screencast demonstrating the user login and main dashboard features.

goal: Build and Deploy a Personal Portfolio Website with React
description: Create a responsive single-page application portfolio to showcase CS projects, using React.js and deploying to Netlify.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Works 9-5 PM weekdays. Available weekends.
baseline.physical_constraints: Limited focus after 8 PM.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use functional components and hooks (no class components).

tasks.description: Install Node.js, create a new React app using Vite, and strip out boilerplate code.
Draw a low-fidelity layout for the Hero section, Projects grid, and Contact footer on paper or Excalidraw.
Create a responsive Navbar component that links to different page sections.
Create a reusable 'Card' component that accepts props for title, description, image, and GitHub link.
Fill the project state with real data from 3 previous university projects and write the 'About Me' text.
Connect GitHub repository to Netlify and trigger the first build.

goal: Optimize Local Machine Learning Model Inference Speed
description: Reduce the latency of a local BERT model's inference by converting it to ONNX format and applying quantization.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Only 8GB VRAM available. CPU is a 6-core processor.
baseline.physical_constraints: High fan noise during testing is disruptive in the shared office.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must maintain at least 95% of original model accuracy.

tasks.description: Run the existing PyTorch model on a set of 100 sample sentences and record the average inference time per sentence.
Use the 'torch.onnx' module to convert the model weights and graph into an .onnx file.
Run the ONNX Runtime quantization script to convert weights from FP32 to INT8.
Test the quantized model against the validation dataset to ensure the F1 score hasn't dropped by more than 5%.
Compare the quantized ONNX inference speed against the baseline recorded in Task 1.

goal: Master Weekly Technical Documentation Writing
description: Improve communication skills by documenting one complex piece of code or system architecture in a clear, accessible README format every week.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Documentation must be completed on Saturday afternoons.
baseline.physical_constraints: Wrist fatigue from coding during the week.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use Markdown. All diagrams must be in Mermaid.js syntax.

tasks.description: Identify one function, class, or logic flow developed during the week that requires explanation.
Write a 2-paragraph summary explaining the 'Why' behind the chosen topic (problem solved, context).
Use Mermaid syntax to visualize how data flows through the component.
Create a code block showing exactly how a developer would call the function or interact with the system.
Run the Markdown file through a linter and a spellchecker.

goal: Secure Personal Linux Server against Unauthorized Access
description: Implement essential security hardening on a new VPS to prevent brute-force attacks and unauthorized root logins.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Using Ubuntu 22.04 LTS. Remote access via SSH only.
baseline.physical_constraints: Risk of locking self out of the server if steps are incorrect.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must disable password authentication entirely. Must use SSH keys.

tasks.description: Create a new user account with a unique name and add it to the 'sudo' group.
Generate an Ed25519 key pair locally and transfer the public key to the server.
Edit /etc/ssh/sshd_config to set 'PermitRootLogin' to 'no' and 'PasswordAuthentication' to 'no'.
Configure the firewall to block all incoming traffic except for SSH (Port 22).
Set up a jail to automatically ban IP addresses after 5 failed login attempts for 24 hours.

goal: Weekly Open Source Contribution Habit
description: Engage with the open-source community by triaging issues or contributing code to projects I use daily.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Limited to Thursday evenings after work.
baseline.physical_constraints: Imposter syndrome/anxiety regarding public code reviews.
baseline.non_negotiables: No contributions to projects without a Code of Conduct. Keep interactions polite.

tasks.description: Browse the 'Help Wanted' or 'Good First Issue' labels in 3 favorite GitHub repositories.
Fork the repository and ensure the project builds and runs all existing tests locally.
Attempt to reproduce the reported issue by writing a minimal failing test case.
Apply a fix for a typo, documentation error, or a minor logic bug identified in Task 3.
Draft a clear PR description following the project's template and link to the relevant issue.

goal: Build a Real-Time Chat App with WebSockets
description: Develop a bidirectional communication app using Node.js and Socket.io that allows multiple users to chat in a single room.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Commute is 30 mins each way.
baseline.physical_constraints: Screen glare issues in the morning; best to code in evening.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include a 'User is Typing' indicator.

tasks.description: Create a basic HTTP server that serves a static 'index.html' file.
Install the library and establish a 'connection' event that logs to the server console.
Write the client-side code to emit a 'chat message' event and the server-side code to broadcast it to all users.
Listen for the 'keypress' event in the input field and emit a 'typing' event to the server.
Use CSS Flexbox to create a scrollable message list and a fixed input bar at the bottom.

goal: Establish a Habit of Nightly Peer Code Reviews
description: Improve code quality and team knowledge sharing by reviewing at least two pending pull requests every evening.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be done after the daily stand-up and before finishing the work day.
baseline.physical_constraints: Mental fatigue high after 4 PM.
baseline.non_negotiables: Comments must be constructive and follow the team style guide.

tasks.description: Scan the team's repository for open pull requests and select two that are under 200 lines of code change.
Read through the changed files to ensure the business logic matches the requirements stated in the linked ticket.
Check that the PR includes new unit tests and that all CI/CD checks have passed.
Provide specific feedback on variable naming, potential refactors, or bugs, then submit the review.

goal: Implement a Production-Ready CI/CD Pipeline for a Microservice
description: Automate the build, test, and deployment process using GitHub Actions and AWS ECR.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Service must stay online during deployment (Zero-downtime).
baseline.physical_constraints: Restricted access to production environment during weekends.
baseline.non_negotiables: Secrets must be stored in GitHub Secrets, never in code.

tasks.description: Create a .github/workflows/main.yml file that triggers on push to the main branch and builds the Docker image.
Add a step to the workflow to run the service's test suite inside the container.
Set up OIDC or IAM credentials to allow GitHub Actions to push images to AWS ECR.
Add the 'docker push' command to the workflow with tags for versioning and 'latest'.
Add a final step to trigger a rolling update on the ECS cluster using the new image.

goal: Solve One LeetCode Problem Weekly in Rust
description: Gain proficiency in Rust by solving algorithmic problems, focusing on ownership and memory safety concepts.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Limited to Sunday mornings.
baseline.physical_constraints: Rust's compiler messages are dense; requires high focus.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use idiomatic Rust (no 'unsafe' blocks).

tasks.description: Choose a 'Medium' problem from the Top Interview 150 list that involves String manipulation or Vectors.
Attempt the solution and resolve all compilation errors related to ownership or lifetimes.
Run the code against the LeetCode test suite and iterate until it passes.
Refactor the working solution to use more functional patterns like .map(), .filter(), or .collect().

goal: Implement a Balanced Binary Search Tree (AVL Tree) from Scratch
description: Deepen understanding of self-balancing trees by implementing a BST with rotation logic to maintain O(log n) height.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Completed in a local IDE using Java.
baseline.physical_constraints: Visualizing rotations can be mentally taxing; best done on a whiteboard first.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include search, insert, and delete operations.

tasks.description: Define the Node class with left, right, and height attributes.
Implement a recursive insert function that places nodes correctly without balancing.
Write the helper functions for 'leftRotate' and 'rightRotate'.
Integrate the height check and rotations into the insert function to handle the 4 imbalance cases.
Implement the search operation and the delete operation with post-deletion balancing.

goal: Daily Review of Official Documentation for a New Framework
description: Stay updated with modern web standards by reading the official documentation for Next.js 15 for 20 minutes every morning.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the morning commute or before the first work meeting.
baseline.physical_constraints: Reading on a small mobile screen may be difficult for complex diagrams.
baseline.non_negotiables: Focus on the 'App Router' features specifically.

tasks.description: Pick one specific chapter (e.g., 'Data Fetching' or 'Server Actions') from the official sidebar.
Read the section thoroughly, paying attention to code examples and warning boxes.
Copy one code example into a personal note-taking app and add comments explaining what each line does.
Identify one way the new concept differs from previous versions or other frameworks.

goal: Daily Practice of Refactoring Legacy Code
description: Improve code maintainability skills by taking a small, messy code snippet daily and applying clean code principles like SOLID or DRY.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the final 30 minutes of the workday.
baseline.physical_constraints: Evening mental fog makes complex architectural changes difficult.
baseline.non_negotiables: The code must remain functional at the end of every session (no broken builds).

tasks.description: Identify a function or class in the current project with a cyclomatic complexity higher than 5.
Create a suite of 3-5 unit tests that describe the current behavior of the snippet to prevent regressions.
Apply a specific pattern such as 'Extract Method', 'Replace Conditional with Polymorphism', or 'Rename Variable'.
Run the tests from Task 2 and remove any temporary comments or dead code.
Write a single sentence in a dev log explaining which Clean Code principle was applied.

goal: Deploy a Secured Dockerized Node.js API
description: Containerize an existing Express.js application and implement industry-standard security hardening for the production image.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use the official Node:alpine base image.
baseline.physical_constraints: Limited bandwidth for downloading large Docker layers.
baseline.non_negotiables: The container must not run as a root user. All secrets must be passed via environment variables.

tasks.description: Write a Dockerfile with separate 'build' and 'production' stages to minimize image size.
Add commands in the Dockerfile to create a system user and set the 'USER' instruction.
Add a HEALTHCHECK instruction to the Dockerfile to monitor the API's 'up' status.
Execute 'docker scout' or 'trivy' to identify vulnerabilities in the image layers.
Tag the final hardened image and push it to a local Docker registry for testing.

goal: Weekly Deep-Dive into Distributed Systems Research
description: Build a high-level understanding of fundamental distributed systems papers (e.g., Paxos, Raft, Dynamo) through weekly reading and summarization.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Completed on Saturday mornings before 11 AM.
baseline.physical_constraints: Paper vocabulary is dense; requires an external dictionary/AI for terminology.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must maintain a permanent bibliography in Obsidian or Notion.

tasks.description: Choose one paper from the 'Distributed Systems Reading List' (e.g., Lamport's Logical Clocks).
Read the first two sections and write down the primary problem the paper aims to solve.
Draw a small flow diagram or state machine representing the core algorithm mentioned (e.g., leader election).
Write a 500-word summary including the solution, its limitations, and one modern use case.

goal: Develop a Basic Software Renderer in C++
description: Build a line-and-triangle renderer from scratch without using APIs like OpenGL or DirectX, learning the mathematics of 3D projection.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must run on a standard Linux/Windows terminal or output to a .ppm file.
baseline.physical_constraints: Geometry math is mentally draining; 45-minute maximum focus per session.
baseline.non_negotiables: No external graphics libraries allowed for the core math/rendering.

tasks.description: Create a simple C++ class to represent a pixel array and save it as a .ppm image file.
Write the function to draw a line between two coordinates (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) in the buffer.
Implement the perspective projection matrix math to transform 3D vertices into 2D screen coordinates.
Connect the projected vertices using the line algorithm from Task 2 to render a 3D wireframe cube.
Implement a basic scanline or half-space algorithm to fill in the triangles of the cube faces.

goal: Daily Practice of SQL Window Functions
description: Master advanced SQL analytics by solving one complex query challenge daily using window functions like RANK, LEAD, and LAG.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Practice on a local PostgreSQL instance or LeetCode SQL section.
baseline.physical_constraints: Limited focus during late-night sessions.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the 'OVER()' clause in every solution.

tasks.description: Find a problem that requires calculating running totals, moving averages, or ranking within partitions.
Write the SQL query to solve the problem, ensuring correct usage of PARTITION BY and ORDER BY within the window.
Manually check the first 5 rows of the output to ensure the window logic (e.g., the rank or the lag) is correct.
Check if adding an index or changing the window partition order improves the query cost.

goal: Build a Custom Layer 7 Load Balancer in Python
description: Develop a tool that distributes incoming HTTP traffic across multiple backend servers using a round-robin algorithm to understand proxying and socket programming.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Limited to Python 3.10 standard library where possible.
baseline.physical_constraints: Debugging network sockets is mentally intensive; session cap of 60 minutes.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the 'socket' library. Must handle at least 3 concurrent backend connections.

tasks.description: Create a configuration file or list containing the IP addresses and port numbers of 3 local test servers.
Write a generator function that yields the next backend server address in a circular fashion.
Write the socket code to receive a request from a client and open a new socket to forward the raw data to a backend.
Implement the return path to send the data received from the backend back to the original client socket.
Log the timestamp, client IP, and target backend server for every processed request to the console.

goal: Daily Cyber-Security CTF Problem Solving
description: Strengthen penetration testing skills by completing one 'Capture The Flag' challenge daily, focusing on web exploitation and cryptography.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done at 8 PM after dinner.
baseline.physical_constraints: Evening screen fatigue; requires blue light filter enabled.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must document the exact steps of the exploit (the 'kill chain').

tasks.description: Select one 'Easy' or 'Medium' challenge from PicoCTF or Hack The Box based on the current weekly theme.
Use tools like Nmap, Gobuster, or browser DevTools to identify open ports or hidden directories.
Apply a specific technique (e.g., SQL Injection, XSS) to bypass authentication or retrieve hidden data.
Record the commands used and the logic behind the successful exploit in a markdown file.

goal: Build a Blockchain Prototype in Go
description: Create a simplified immutable ledger that implements cryptographic hashing, block linking, and a proof-of-work mining mechanism.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use standard Go libraries for crypto/sha256.
baseline.physical_constraints: New to Go concurrency patterns; requires careful reading of documentation.
baseline.non_negotiables: Each block must be cryptographically linked to the previous one. Data must be immutable once mined.

tasks.description: Create a struct in Go representing a Block with fields for Index, Timestamp, Data, PreviousHash, Hash, and Nonce.
Write a function that concatenates block fields into a string and returns its SHA-256 hash.
Write a function that hardcodes and returns the first block (index 0) of the chain.
Create a Proof-of-Work function that increments a Nonce until the resulting hash starts with a specific number of zeros (difficulty).
Write a loop that iterates through the chain to verify that each block's 'PreviousHash' matches the actual hash of the preceding block.

goal: Weekly Technical Mock Interview Practice
description: Simulate a real-world software engineering interview environment every Saturday to improve technical communication and live coding performance.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Scheduled for Saturday mornings at 10 AM.
baseline.physical_constraints: High performance anxiety; requires 10 minutes of breathing exercises beforehand.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must code in a 'blank' environment without auto-complete or AI assistance.

tasks.description: Connect with a peer on Pramp or use a randomized generator to get a 'Medium' difficulty behavioral or technical prompt.
Spend 5 minutes asking clarifying questions about inputs, edge cases, and constraints before touching the keyboard.
Solve the problem while explaining the thought process out loud to the interviewer or a recording device.
Watch the recording or review peer feedback to identify 2 specific areas for improvement (e.g., filler words, slow Big O analysis).

goal: Set Up a Monitoring Dashboard for a Home Server
description: Deploy a visual monitoring stack to track CPU, Memory, and Disk health of a Raspberry Pi or local PC in real-time.

baseline.physical_constraints: Local network firewall issues might require port-forwarding troubleshooting.
