Install and start Node Exporter on the host machine to collect hardware-level metrics.
Start a Grafana container and map its internal port 3000 to the host.
Inside Grafana, add the local Prometheus container address as a 'Prometheus' data source.
Import the 'Node Exporter Full' dashboard (ID 1860) to visualize the host metrics.

goal: Build a Pathfinding Visualizer for Dijkstra and A*
description: Create a web-based tool that visualizes how Dijkstra's and A* algorithms find the shortest path on a 2D grid with obstacles.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must be built using Vanilla JavaScript or React.
baseline.physical_constraints: Geometric coordinate logic can be confusing; requires a clear mental state.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must allow the user to click-and-drag to draw walls. Must show the 'visited' nodes animation.

tasks.description: Create a 2D array and render it as a grid of <div> elements with 'Start' and 'End' nodes.
Add event listeners to grid cells to toggle a 'wall' state on mouse click or drag.
Write the Dijkstra algorithm logic that calculates distances and tracks the 'previousNode' for backtracking.
Use CSS transitions and 'setTimeout' to change the background color of visited nodes sequentially.
Modify the logic to include Manhattan distance (h-cost) to turn the Dijkstra search into an A* search.

goal: Daily Review of System Design Patterns
description: Internalize distributed system patterns (Circuit Breaker, Idempotency, CQRS) through daily targeted reading.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Performed during the lunch break (12 PM - 1 PM).
baseline.physical_constraints: Limited to reading on a tablet or phone.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must explain the concept to a 'rubber duck' (out loud) to verify comprehension.

tasks.description: Pick one pattern from the 'Cloud Design Patterns' catalog (e.g., Retry, Throttling).
Identify the Problem, Solution, and at least 2 potential drawbacks of the pattern.
Explain the pattern out loud as if explaining it to a junior engineer.
Find one modern tool that implements this pattern (e.g., Netflix Hystrix for Circuit Breaker).

goal: Build a Multi-Threaded Port Scanner in C
description: Create a high-performance C program that scans a range of TCP ports using POSIX threads to understand concurrency and networking.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must compile with GCC on Linux.
baseline.physical_constraints: C pointer errors (Segfaults) are frustrating; requires an environment with GDB installed.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use 'pthread' library. Must handle 'Connection Refused' errors gracefully.

tasks.description: Write a basic C loop that tries to 'connect()' to a range of ports (1-1024) one by one.
Refactor the code to create 10 worker threads that print their unique Thread IDs.
Create a shared 'port_counter' and a mutex to ensure threads pick unique ports to scan without duplication.
Set the socket to non-blocking mode and use 'select()' to handle timeouts for slow-responding ports.
Run the scanner against a local target and measure the total execution time compared to Task 1.

goal: Weekly Practice of Writing Technical Blog Posts
description: Improve technical writing by publishing one short, high-quality blog post about a concept learned during the week.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Drafting on Friday, publishing on Sunday.
baseline.physical_constraints: Typing fatigue; recommends using a mechanical keyboard.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include at least one code snippet and one visual aid.

tasks.description: Review the week's study notes and select one topic that was difficult to understand at first.
Write the core section of the post explaining the breakthrough or the logic in simple terms.
Write and test a minimal, copy-pasteable code example that illustrates the concept.
Review for flow, add a feature image, and publish to Hashnode or Dev.to.

goal: Migrate a Monolithic App to Serverless Functions
description: Extract two high-traffic endpoints from a monolithic Express app and redeploy them as AWS Lambda functions.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use the Serverless Framework or AWS SAM.
baseline.physical_constraints: IAM permissions can be a 'rabbit hole'; set a hard stop for troubleshooting.
baseline.non_negotiables: The database connection must be shared (RDS/DynamoDB).

tasks.description: Identify the two endpoints with the highest latency and map their external dependencies.
Setup a serverless.yml file and configure the provider as AWS with the correct region.
Rewrite the Express route logic into a standalone handler(event, context) function.
Map the HTTP events in serverless.yml to the new Lambda functions.
Update the frontend or client to point to the new Lambda URL for the specific endpoints.

goal: Master the Basics of Assembly Language (x86-64)
description: Learn to write and debug low-level programs by implementing basic arithmetic and control flow directly in x86-64 assembly.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use NASM assembler and GCC for linking on a Linux environment.
baseline.physical_constraints: Mental exhaustion occurs quickly when tracking register states; sessions limited to 45 minutes.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the System V AMD64 ABI calling convention for function calls.

tasks.description: Write a program using system calls (sys_write and sys_exit) to print a string to the terminal.
Create a function that takes two integers via registers, adds them, and returns the result in RAX.
Write an assembly routine that calculates the sum of the first N integers using a loop and conditional jumps (cmp/jne).
Use GDB to step through the loop line-by-line and observe how the values in RDI and RAX change.
Implement a function that uses the stack for local variable storage, correctly managing the base pointer (RBP).

goal: Weekly Audit of Personal Cloud Security Settings
description: Ensure personal cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP) remains secure by performing a weekly manual check of IAM roles and security groups.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Performed on Monday mornings before starting deep work.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires high attention to detail to spot misconfigurations.
baseline.non_negotiables: Any '0.0.0.0/0' inbound rules on non-web ports must be flagged and removed immediately.

tasks.description: Check the list of active IAM access keys and identify any that have not been used in the last 30 days.
Review all active Security Groups for open ports, specifically looking for SSH (22) or RDP (3389) open to the world.
Verify that no S3 buckets are set to 'Public Access' unless explicitly intended for static hosting.
Log the findings and any remediation steps taken into a private security journal.

goal: Build a Custom Web Scraper for Market Analysis
description: Create a Python-based scraper that extracts pricing data from a target e-commerce site and saves it to a structured JSON format.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use BeautifulSoup4 and Requests libraries.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires sitting at a desk for long periods; remember to stand every 20 mins.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must respect the site's robots.txt and implement a 2-second delay between requests.

tasks.description: Use browser DevTools to identify the HTML tags and classes used for product names and prices.
Write a Python script that fetches the HTML of a single product page and prints the raw content.
Use BeautifulSoup to parse the HTML and isolate the specific product name and price strings.
Wrap the extraction logic in a loop that iterates through a list of 10 URLs with a 2-second sleep between each.
Modify the script to save the final list of dictionaries to a file named 'market_data.json'.

goal: Daily Review of Distributed Systems Latency Numbers
description: Internalize 'Latency Numbers Every Programmer Should Know' (e.g., L1 cache vs. Disk seek) through daily recall.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Review done during the morning commute or first cup of coffee.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires visualizing scale (nanoseconds to seconds).
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to state the order of magnitude for each number.

tasks.description: Try to list the latencies for: L1 cache, Main Memory, SSD random read, and a round-trip within a data center from memory.
Compare the recalled numbers against the official interactive chart and note any errors in magnitude.
Think of one system design scenario where these numbers matter (e.g., why caching is necessary for database queries).

goal: Implement a Generic Linked List in Rust
description: Understand Rust's memory management by implementing a singly linked list that can hold any data type using generics and Box pointers.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must not use 'unsafe' blocks. Must follow the 'Learning Rust With Entirely Too Many Linked Lists' methodology.
baseline.physical_constraints: Wrestling with the borrow checker can lead to frustration; take a break if stuck on a compiler error for 15 mins.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement push, pop, and peek methods.

tasks.task: Define the Node and List Structs
tasks.description: Create the basic structure using 'Option<Box<Node<T>>>' to handle null pointers safely.
Write the logic to add a new element to the front of the list, transferring ownership of the current head.
Write the logic to remove the head of the list and return its value, using 'Option::take()'.
Implement methods to return immutable and mutable references to the head value without removing it.
Manually implement the Drop trait to iteratively clean up nodes and prevent stack overflow from recursive drops.

goal: Build a Real-Time Distributed Key-Value Store
description: Develop a simplified version of Redis that supports concurrent reads/writes and basic networking using Go and TCP sockets.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must run on a local network. Using Go 1.21+.
baseline.physical_constraints: High cognitive load when debugging race conditions; sessions limited to 90 minutes.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be thread-safe. Must support 'SET', 'GET', and 'DEL' commands.

tasks.description: Create a thread-safe wrapper around a Go map using sync.RWMutex to store string keys and values.
Write a parser for a subset of the REdis Serialization Protocol (RESP) to handle incoming command strings.
Implement a network listener that accepts multiple concurrent TCP connections and spawns a goroutine for each.
Link the TCP listener and the parser to the storage engine to perform real data operations.
Write a background worker that appends every write command to a 'log.aof' file for crash recovery.

goal: Daily Technical Paper Skimming Habit
description: Stay at the forefront of AI and CS by skimming the latest pre-prints on arXiv every morning.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Performed while commuting or during breakfast.
baseline.physical_constraints: Mobile device reading; focus on figures and abstracts.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must save at least one paper per week to the 'Deep Read' list.

tasks.description: Open the arXiv 'recent' page for Computer Science and scroll through the last 24 hours of titles.
Read the abstract of the most relevant paper and look at 'Figure 1' or the 'Results' table.
Identify one cited work in the paper that is a foundational precursor to the current research.

goal: Optimize a Web Application's Critical Rendering Path
description: Improve the Lighthouse performance score of a personal site from <70 to >90 by optimizing asset loading.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Testing on a simulated 'Slow 4G' mobile connection.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires multiple browser restarts for clean cache testing.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must not remove any existing visual features or images.

tasks.description: Run a Lighthouse audit and identify the top 3 'opportunities' (e.g., render-blocking resources).
Convert all images to WebP format and implement 'loading=lazy' for off-screen assets.
Inline the critical CSS required for the above-the-fold content and defer the rest using a media='print' trick or async load.
Enable Gzip or Brotli compression on the server and ensure all JS/CSS files are minified.
Perform the final Lighthouse audit to confirm the new score meets the target of >90.

goal: Weekly Documentation of Home Network Architecture
description: Maintain an accurate technical map of all devices, static IPs, and VLANs in the home lab to assist in future troubleshooting.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Sunday afternoon activity.
baseline.physical_constraints: May require physically checking labels on hardware (routers/switches).
baseline.non_negotiables: All documentation must be stored in a centralized wiki or git repo.

tasks.description: Login to the router and check the 'Connected Devices' list against the existing documentation.
Modify the Mermaid.js or Draw.io diagram to reflect any changes in VLAN tagging or port forwarding.
Record the current firmware versions for the gateway, switch, and access points.

goal: Build a CLI Tool for Automated Backups to AWS S3
description: Create a Python script that zips a specified local directory and uploads it to a private S3 bucket with a timestamped filename.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Boto3 library. Running on Windows/Linux.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires managing AWS credentials securely.
baseline.non_negotiables: Backups must be encrypted on the client side before upload.

tasks.description: Create a new private S3 bucket and an IAM user with 'PutObject' permissions only.
Write a Python function using the 'shutil' library to compress a target folder into a .zip archive.
Use the 'cryptography' library to encrypt the .zip file using a local secret key.
Use Boto3 to upload the encrypted archive to the specified S3 bucket with a 'yyyy-mm-dd_backup.zip' name.
Add logic to delete the local temporary .zip files and log the success/failure to a local 'backup.log' file.

goal: Pass the Cisco Certified Network Associate (CCNA) Exam
description: A structured daily study routine to cover the 200-301 exam topics, including IP connectivity, security fundamentals, and automation.

baseline.fixed_constraints: 90 minutes available every weekday evening.
baseline.physical_constraints: Eye strain from reading PDFs; must use printed books or e-ink where possible.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use Packet Tracer for all lab exercises. No skipping the 'Subnetting' chapter.

tasks.description: Read one sub-chapter of the Official Cert Guide (OCG) covering a specific topic like OSPF or STP.
Open Packet Tracer and configure the topology relevant to the day's reading (e.g., 'Configure Router-on-a-Stick').
Review the Anki deck for command syntax and port numbers.
Take a 10-question practice quiz on the specific domain studied.

goal: Analyze Network Traffic with Wireshark
description: Capture and dissect a specific protocol exchange (DNS and HTTP) to understand the packet structure and flow.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must run Wireshark with appropriate permissions.
baseline.physical_constraints: Analyzing hex dumps is mentally taxing.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must identify the 3-way handshake explicitly.

tasks.description: Start a Wireshark capture on the main interface and visit a non-HTTPS website (e.g., example.com).
Apply a display filter to isolate traffic to the target IP address.
Locate the SYN, SYN-ACK, and ACK packets establishing the connection.
Expand the details pane to inspect the Ethernet, IP, and TCP headers for a single data packet.
Remove the IP filter and search for the DNS 'A' record query that preceded the HTTP connection.

goal: Master IP Subnetting Speed
description: Achieve the ability to calculate network ID, broadcast IP, and host range for any CIDR block in under 30 seconds.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Practice during commute or idle time.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: No calculators allowed. Mental math or scratchpad only.

tasks.description: Use a subnetting practice app or website to generate 5 random CIDR questions (e.g., /26, /19).
Convert the last octet of the IP address and the Subnet Mask into binary for one difficult problem.
Calculate the first usable host, last usable host, and broadcast address for all 5 problems.
Check answers against the tool and analyze any off-by-one errors.

goal: Configure a VLAN-Segmented Home Network
description: Isolate IoT devices from the main PC network using a managed switch and a router supporting 802.1Q VLAN tagging.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Hardware: Ubiquiti or generic managed switch.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires crawling under desk to recable devices.
baseline.non_negotiables: Main PC must retain internet access at all times.

tasks.description: Create a table defining VLAN IDs (e.g., 10 for Trusted, 20 for IoT) and their corresponding subnets.
Log into the router and create virtual interfaces for each VLAN with the gateway IP.
Set the uplink port on the managed switch to 'Trunk' or 'All' mode to carry tagged traffic.
Set specific ports on the switch to 'Access' mode for the designated VLAN ID (e.g., Port 4 -> VLAN 20).
Add rules to the router to block traffic from VLAN 20 (IoT) to VLAN 10 (Trusted).

goal: Design and Deploy a Zero Trust Network Architecture for a Small Office
description: Transition a flat office network to a Zero Trust model where no device is trusted by default, utilizing identity-based access and micro-segmentation.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must utilize existing hardware (managed switches and a pfSense router).
baseline.physical_constraints: Implementation must occur after hours (6 PM - 10 PM) to avoid business disruption.
baseline.non_negotiables: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) must be required for all VPN access.

tasks.description: Map all network devices and document the necessary data flows between departments (e.g., HR to Payroll server).
Configure an OpenID Connect (OIDC) or RADIUS server to act as the single source of truth for user identities.
Create isolated VLANs for different trust levels (Users, Servers, Guest, IoT) and apply strict inter-VLAN firewall rules.

tasks.task: Set Up WireGuard VPN with MFA
tasks.description: Deploy a WireGuard VPN tunnel for remote access that requires an MFA token for session establishment.
Configure an 802.1X policy that checks if a connecting device has a valid certificate and up-to-date antivirus.

goal: Maintain Daily Network Health via Monitoring
description: Sustain 99.9% uptime by proactively reviewing network metrics and logs for anomalies before they lead to outages.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Review performed at the start of the workday (8:30 AM).
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires high focus on time-series graphs.
baseline.non_negotiables: All high-severity alerts must be acknowledged within 30 minutes of the check.

tasks.description: Analyze Grafana or Zabbix dashboards for CPU usage, memory, and interface errors on core routers.
Search the last 24 hours of centralized Syslog entries for 'Critical' or 'Emergency' level messages.
Run a multi-target ping test to common DNS servers (1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8) to check for jitter or packet loss.
Verify that the automated nightly backup of device configurations (e.g., via Oxidized) was successful.

goal: Configure BGP Peering in a Multi-Homed Lab
description: Establish External BGP (eBGP) peering between two Autonomous Systems (AS) to learn about route propagation and path selection.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Using virtual routers (GNS3 or EVE-NG).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the 'AS Path' attribute to influence routing decisions.

tasks.description: Assign private AS numbers and peer IP addresses for the 'Provider' and 'Customer' routers.
Configure the 'neighbor' command on both routers to initiate the eBGP session.
Use the 'network' command to advertise local loopback networks into the BGP table.
Configure a route-map to prepend the local AS number multiple times for outgoing advertisements to test path preference.

goal: Weekly Network Protocol Deep-Dive
description: Achieve a master-level understanding of core Internet protocols by reading one RFC (Request for Comments) per week.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Reading sessions occur on Sunday mornings.
baseline.physical_constraints: RFCs are plain text and very long; requires a highlighter tool.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must focus on the 'Header Format' and 'State Machine' sections of each RFC.

tasks.description: Choose a foundational RFC (e.g., RFC 791 for IP, RFC 793 for TCP) from a curated list.
Sketch the packet header on a piece of paper, labeling every field and its bit length.
Document the transitions between states (e.g., TCP LISTEN to SYN_SENT) based on specific flags.
Write a 3-bullet point list of the protocol's known limitations or security vulnerabilities as described in the RFC.

goal: Build an Automated Network Device Inventory Script
description: Create a Python tool that uses Netmiko to SSH into multiple devices and pull serial numbers and hardware models into a spreadsheet.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Python 3.x and the Netmiko library.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires access to the network management VLAN.
baseline.non_negotiables: Credentials must be prompted at runtime, never stored in the script.

tasks.description: Install Netmiko and create a text file containing the IP addresses of the target devices.
Write the core loop to SSH into a single device and execute a 'show version' command.
Use Regular Expressions (re) to parse the Serial Number and Model Name from the raw command output.
Modify the script to append the extracted data into a CSV file after each device connection.
Add try-except blocks to catch 'AuthenticationFailed' or 'NetmikoTimeoutException' to prevent script crashes.

goal: Build a Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) with Public and Private Subnets
description: Create a secure cloud network environment in AWS or GCP that isolates sensitive database servers from the public internet using a NAT Gateway.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use the cloud provider's web console (no CLI/Terraform yet).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Database subnet must have zero direct inbound routes from the internet.

tasks.description: Create a new VPC with a primary IPv4 CIDR block of 10.0.0.0/16.
Provision two subnets: one public (10.0.1.0/24) and one private (10.0.2.0/24).
Attach an Internet Gateway (IGW) to the VPC and add a route in the public route table (0.0.0.0/0 -> IGW).
Provision a NAT Gateway in the public subnet and update the private route table to route external traffic through it.
Create a security group for the private subnet that only allows inbound traffic on Port 3306 from the public subnet's IP range.

goal: Daily Review of Network Security Vulnerabilities (CVEs)
description: Maintain awareness of emerging threats by reviewing new Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) related to networking hardware and protocols.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the morning news check.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires high critical thinking to assess relevance to personal lab.
baseline.non_negotiables: Any vulnerability affecting 'Active' hardware in the lab must be logged for patching.

tasks.description: Review the National Vulnerability Database (NVD) or a curated security feed like 'CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities'.
Cross-reference the vendors mentioned in high-severity CVEs with the local network inventory (Cisco, Ubiquiti, Synology).
Record any relevant CVE IDs in a 'Patch Tracker' and assign a priority level based on exposure.

goal: Set Up a Site-to-Site VPN Between Home and Lab
description: Create a permanent, encrypted tunnel between two geographically separate networks using IPsec to allow seamless resource sharing.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Both sites use static public IPs or dynamic DNS. Both routers must support IPsec.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires simultaneous access to both network consoles.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use AES-256 encryption or higher. Pre-shared keys must be at least 32 characters.

tasks.description: Configure the IKE (Internet Key Exchange) policy on both routers (Encryption: AES-256, Hash: SHA-256, DH Group: 14).
Define the IPsec security association (SA) parameters for data encapsulation.

tasks.task: Define Crypto Map and Traffic Selectors
tasks.description: Specify the local and remote subnets that are allowed to traverse the VPN tunnel.
Create firewall rules to allow UDP 500/4500 and ESP traffic. Configure NAT Exemption to prevent translation of internal VPN traffic.
Perform an ICMP ping from a host in Site A to a host in Site B using internal IP addresses.

goal: Weekly Network Documentation Update
description: Maintain a 'living' network diagram and IP address management (IPAM) sheet to prevent IP conflicts and improve disaster recovery.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done on Friday afternoons. Using Lucidchart or Visio.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: All changes made during the week must be reflected in the 'Current' version.

tasks.description: Review the DHCP server lease table for any unexpected devices or new static assignments.
Verify if any cables were moved or new switches added and update the physical topology diagram.
Check if any administrative passwords or SSH keys are due for rotation according to the 90-day policy.

goal: Build a Simple Software-Defined Network (SDN) Controller
description: Develop a Python script that uses the Northbound API of a controller (like Ryu or Floodlight) to dynamically push flow entries to OpenvSwitch.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Using Mininet for network emulation. Python 3.x.
baseline.physical_constraints: SDN logic is abstract; requires a whiteboard to visualize flow tables.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to block a specific MAC address via an API call.

tasks.description: Launch a single switch, three-host topology in Mininet connected to a remote controller.
Write a Python script that uses the 'requests' library to query the controller's REST API for flow table stats.
Implement a function that sends a POST request to the controller to add a high-priority 'DROP' flow for a specific IP.
Verify the flow entry in the switch and confirm that the targeted host can no longer communicate.
Add a function to clear the specific flow entry and restore network connectivity.

goal: Master OSI Model Layers and Encapsulation Logic
description: Develop a deep understanding of how data moves through the stack by studying one OSI layer per day and tracing the encapsulation process.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Study sessions occur during the morning commute (30 minutes).
baseline.physical_constraints: Limited to reading or watching videos on a mobile device.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to name the Data Unit (PDU) for each layer without looking.

tasks.description: Read the technical specifications and primary functions of the designated 'Layer of the Day' (e.g., Layer 3 - Network).
Identify and write down the Protocol Data Unit (e.g., Segment, Packet, Frame) associated with the current layer.
Draw or mentally map how a piece of data is wrapped with headers from the Application layer down to the current layer.

goal: Deploy a Redundant Load Balancer with HAProxy and Keepalived
description: Configure a high-availability entry point for a web cluster that automatically fails over to a backup node if the primary node goes down.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use two Ubuntu Linux virtual machines.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires stable local network connectivity for heartbeat signals.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a Virtual IP (VIP) that floats between nodes.

tasks.description: Install the HAProxy package and verify that the service starts correctly on both virtual machines.
Update haproxy.cfg to point to dummy backend servers and implement basic HTTP health checks.
Install Keepalived and configure the VRRP instance to define the 'MASTER' and 'BACKUP' roles.
Stop the HAProxy service on the MASTER node and observe if the VIP moves to the BACKUP node.
Record the final working configuration and VIP addresses in the infrastructure log.

goal: Weekly Network Traffic Profiling with Ntopng
description: Maintain a secure home network by performing a weekly analysis of top talkers, application usage, and potential data leaks.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Analysis performed on Saturday mornings.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires access to the local monitoring dashboard.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must investigate any traffic to unknown foreign IP addresses.

tasks.description: Identify the top 5 devices by bandwidth usage in the last 7 days and verify they are authorized.
Review the percentage of traffic dedicated to specific protocols (e.g., HTTPS, BitTorrent, DNS).
Analyze the map of destination countries for outgoing traffic and flag any unexpected regions.

goal: Build a Custom Network Speed-Test CLI in Python
description: Create a tool that measures upload/download speeds and latency, logging results to a file for long-term ISP performance tracking.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use the 'speedtest-cli' library as a backend.
baseline.physical_constraints: Test results may be inaccurate if other devices are streaming on the network.
baseline.non_negotiables: Results must be exported to a CSV file with a timestamp.

tasks.description: Set up a virtual environment and install the speedtest-cli and pandas libraries.
Write a function to initialize the Speedtest object, find the best server, and run download/upload tests.
Format the test results into a dictionary with a timestamp and append it to a CSV file.
Add logic to handle network timeouts or 'No Server Found' errors gracefully.

goal: Daily Practice of IPv6 Addressing and Notation
description: Gain fluency in IPv6 by practicing address compression, expansion, and identifying address types (Link-Local, Global Unicast).

baseline.fixed_constraints: Completed during morning coffee.
baseline.physical_constraints: Long hex strings require careful transcription.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the double-colon rule correctly for zero compression.

tasks.description: Take 3 fully-expanded IPv6 addresses and apply the zero-omission and double-colon rules to compress them.
Identify if a given IPv6 address is a Global Unicast, Link-Local (fe80::), or Unique Local (fc00::).
Write out the first 16 bits in binary for a Global Unicast address to understand the prefix range.

goal: Master Wireless LAN (WLAN) Configuration and Security
description: Establish a consistent routine for studying 802.11 standards, frame types, and WPA3 security protocols to prepare for wireless certification.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Limited to 1 hour after work on weekdays.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a dedicated wireless access point for testing (no messing with the home production Wi-Fi).

tasks.description: Study the modulation, frequency, and maximum speeds of a specific standard (e.g., 802.11ax vs. 802.11ac).
Use a wireless adapter in monitor mode to capture 802.11 management frames (Beacons, Probes) in Wireshark.
Analyze the 4-way WPA2/WPA3 handshake in a packet capture to understand key exchange.

goal: Deploy an OpenVPN Server on a Raspberry Pi
description: Set up a private VPN server to allow secure remote access to the home network from public hotspots.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Raspberry Pi 4 with Raspberry Pi OS installed.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires physical access to the router for port forwarding.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use certificate-based authentication (no shared secrets only).

tasks.description: Execute the automated PiVPN installation script and select OpenVPN as the protocol.
Log into the home router and forward UDP port 1194 to the Raspberry Pi's static local IP.
Create a new .ovpn profile for a mobile device and export it via secure copy (SCP).
Disconnect the mobile device from Wi-Fi, enable cellular data, and initiate the VPN connection.

goal: Weekly Linux Network Troubleshooting Drills
description: Practice diagnosing common connectivity issues using standard Linux CLI tools (ip, nmcli, ss, traceroute).

baseline.fixed_constraints: Saturday morning sessions (45 minutes).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must document the exact command used to find the 'root cause'.

tasks.description: Use the 'ip addr' and 'ip link' commands to verify interface status, MTU, and IP assignments.
Analyze the output of 'ip route' to determine the default gateway and any specific static routes.
Use the 'ss -tulpn' command to identify all listening ports and the processes associated with them.
Perform a 'mtr' (My Traceroute) to a public target and analyze each hop for packet loss.

goal: Implement a Network-Wide Ad Blocker (Pi-hole)
description: Deploy a DNS-based ad blocker to protect all devices on the network from tracking and advertisements.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Requires a Raspberry Pi or a Linux VM.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires administrative access to the router's DNS settings.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must not break DNS resolution for critical work devices.

tasks.description: Run the official Pi-hole installation script and configure a static IP for the server.
Select a secure upstream provider (e.g., Unbound or Cloudflare) within the Pi-hole settings.
Change the LAN DNS settings on the home router to point to the Pi-hole server's IP.
Add one additional 'Adlist' from a community-verified source and trigger a gravity update.

goal: Daily Practice of Wireshark Display Filter Syntax
description: Master the syntax for complex display filters to quickly find specific traffic in large capture files.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done at the start of the study session (10 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use logical operators (&&, ||, !) in every practice session.

tasks.description: Write a filter to find all HTTP POST requests or all DNS responses with a specific error code.
Combine two filters (e.g., 'ip.addr == X and tcp.port == Y') to narrow down a specific conversation.

goal: Master Big O Notation and Complexity Analysis
description: Develop the ability to instinctively identify the Time and Space complexity of any given code snippet through daily practice.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Practice during lunch break (20 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must differentiate between Average Case and Worst Case scenarios.

tasks.description: Select a random function from a 'Cracking the Coding Interview' solution repository.
Identify the number of iterations relative to input 'N' and note any nested operations.
If the function is recursive, draw the first 3 levels of the recursion tree to determine the branching factor.
Write down the final Big O notation for Time and Space (e.g., O(N log N)).
Determine if specific inputs (e.g., sorted array) change the complexity (Best Case vs. Worst Case).

goal: Implement a Resizable Dynamic Array (Vector) from Scratch
description: Build a custom `ArrayList` class in C++ or Java to understand memory allocation, resizing logic, and amortized analysis.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use raw arrays/pointers, not built-in vector libraries.
baseline.physical_constraints: Debugging memory leaks in C++ can be tedious.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement the geometric resizing strategy (doubling capacity).

tasks.description: Create a class with a private array pointer, `size` (current elements), and `capacity` (total slots).
Write the method to add an element to the end of the array if `size < capacity`.
Write a private `resize()` method that creates a new array of double the capacity, copies elements, and deletes the old array.
Add methods to access or modify elements at a specific index, throwing an error if out of bounds.
Write a short comment block explaining why appending N elements takes O(N) time total, not O(N^2).

goal: Build an LRU (Least Recently Used) Cache
description: Combine a Hash Map and a Doubly Linked List to create an O(1) access cache with eviction policies.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must pass the LeetCode #146 test suite or local unit tests.
baseline.physical_constraints: Pointer manipulation in Doubly Linked Lists is error-prone.
baseline.non_negotiables: Get and Put operations must both be O(1).

tasks.description: Create a Doubly Linked List Node class containing `key`, `value`, `prev`, and `next` pointers.
Write helper functions to add a node right after the 'head' dummy and remove a node from anywhere in the list.
Check the HashMap for the key. If found, move the corresponding node to the front (head) and return value.
Add new key-value pairs. If capacity is exceeded, remove the LRU node (tail's previous) and delete from Map.
Execute a sequence of Get/Put operations and verify the state of the cache matches expected results.

goal: Daily Graph Traversal Visualization (BFS/DFS)
description: Develop intuition for graph algorithms by manually tracing Breadth-First and Depth-First searches on random graphs.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done on a whiteboard or tablet.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must track the 'Queue' (for BFS) or 'Stack' (for DFS) state explicitly.

tasks.description: Sketch a graph with 6-8 nodes and 10 edges, including at least one cycle.
Choose either BFS or DFS and a starting node (e.g., Node A).
Step through the algorithm, updating the 'Visited' set and the Queue/Stack data structure on paper for each step.
Identify 'Tree Edges' vs 'Back Edges' (for DFS) during the traversal.
Run the same graph/algorithm in a visualizer tool (like VisuAlgo) to verify the manual trace.

goal: Implement a Binary Heap (Priority Queue)
description: Construct a Min-Heap using an array-based representation to understand tree-to-array mapping and the 'sift-up/sift-down' operations.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python or Java.
baseline.physical_constraints: Index arithmetic (parent/child calculations) requires care.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement both `insert` and `extract_min`.

tasks.description: Write functions to calculate `parent(i)`, `left_child(i)`, and `right_child(i)` for a given index `i`.
Write the logic to swap a node with its parent if the heap property is violated (used during insertion).
Add the new element to the end of the array and call the 'Bubble Up' function.
Write the logic to swap a node with its smallest child to restore heap property (used during extraction).
Save the root value, move the last element to the root, remove the last element, and call 'Bubble Down'.

goal: Build an Autocomplete System using a Trie (Prefix Tree)
description: Implement a Trie data structure to efficiently store a dictionary and return all words starting with a given prefix.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python 3.x.
baseline.physical_constraints: Recursive logic for tree traversal requires high focus.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must support insert, search, and starts_with operations.

tasks.description: Create a class containing a dictionary of children and a boolean 'is_end_of_word'.
Write a method to iterate through characters in a word, creating new nodes as needed.
Write the 'starts_with' method to navigate the Trie and verify if a path exists for a string.
Implement a DFS helper function to find all words under a specific prefix node.

goal: Daily Practice of Linked List Reversal Logic
description: Internalize pointer manipulation by manually tracing and coding a linked list reversal every day.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Morning routine (15 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: Avoid using IDE auto-complete.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement both iterative and recursive approaches during the 14 days.

tasks.description: Draw 4 nodes and trace the 'prev', 'curr', and 'next' pointers through a reversal loop.
Implement the `reverseList` function without referring to previous notes.

goal: Implement a Thread-Safe Hash Map with Chaining
description: Create a robust hash table that handles collisions via linked lists and uses locks for thread safety.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Java or C++ (with pthreads).
baseline.physical_constraints: Concurrency bugs can be difficult to reproduce.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a custom hash function, not the built-in language hash.

tasks.description: Implement the 'polynomial rolling hash' or 'modulo' method for string keys.
Initialize an array of 'buckets', where each bucket is a Singly Linked List.
Write the 'put' method to append entries to the linked list at a specific bucket index.
Apply a mutex/lock to each individual bucket (lock-striping) rather than the whole table.

goal: Weekly Deep-Dive into Red-Black Tree Balancing
description: Internalize the 4 rotation/recoloring cases of Red-Black Trees to understand how self-balancing works in production systems.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Sunday morning study (1 hour).
baseline.physical_constraints: Visualizing 'double-black' nodes is mentally taxing.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to explain the 5 Red-Black properties.

tasks.description: Recite the 5 rules of Red-Black trees (e.g., Root is always black, No two red nodes in a row).
Insert 5 random numbers into a tree on paper and apply the recoloring/rotation rules for each.

goal: Build a Graph Adjacency List and Implement Dijkstra's Algorithm
description: Construct a weighted graph and use a Priority Queue to find the shortest path between nodes.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Java or Python.
baseline.physical_constraints: Debugging priority queue updates can be complex.
baseline.non_negotiables: Graph must support directed, weighted edges.

tasks.description: Represent the graph as a Map of Lists where each entry contains a neighbor and a weight.
Setup a min-heap to store (distance, node) pairs for the greedy selection step.
Write the loop that updates neighbor distances if a shorter path is found via the current node.
Use a 'parent' map to backtrack from the destination node to the source node.

goal: Master Merge Sort Implementation from Scratch
description: Understand and code the Divide and Conquer strategy of Merge Sort without relying on built-in sort functions.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Python or C++.
baseline.physical_constraints: Recursion tracing requires a large whiteboard or paper.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement the recursive solution, not iterative.

tasks.description: Draw the full splitting tree for an array of 8 random numbers down to single elements.
Write a function that takes two sorted arrays and combines them into a single sorted array.
Write the main function to recursively split the input array until the base case (length <= 1) is reached.
Call the 'Merge' helper inside the recursive function to sort the arrays as they unwind.
Annotate the code to explain why the splitting is log(n) and the merging is n, resulting in O(n log n).

goal: Solve the 0/1 Knapsack Problem using Dynamic Programming
description: Learn to optimize resource allocation by implementing the classic Knapsack problem using a 2D DP table.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Java or Python.
baseline.physical_constraints: Drawing the 2D grid is essential for understanding state transitions.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement the Bottom-Up (Tabulation) approach.

tasks.description: Write down mathematically what `dp[i][w]` represents (max value using first `i` items with capacity `w`).
Create a 2D array of size (N+1) x (W+1) filled with zeros.
Write the nested loops to iterate through items and weights, applying the 'include' vs 'exclude' logic.
Manually fill out the table on paper for a small input (3 items, Capacity=5) and compare with the code's output.
Refactor the solution to use a 1D array instead of a 2D array to reduce space complexity to O(W).

goal: Daily Two-Pointer Technique Practice
description: Build intuition for array manipulation problems by solving one 'Two Pointer' problem daily (e.g., Container With Most Water, 3Sum).

baseline.fixed_constraints: Practice during morning coffee (20 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.

tasks.description: Select a problem tagged 'Two Pointers' on LeetCode.
Draw an array on paper and indicate where the 'Left' and 'Right' pointers start and how they move.
Write the `while (left < right)` loop and the internal logic.

goal: Implement Union-Find (Disjoint Set) with Path Compression
description: Create a `UnionFind` class to efficiently track connected components in a graph.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python or C++.
baseline.physical_constraints: Visualizing tree flattening (path compression) helps.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement both Path Compression and Union by Rank/Size.

tasks.description: Initialize two arrays: `parent` (to store parent nodes) and `rank` (to store tree height), where `parent[i] = i` initially.
Write the recursive `find(x)` function that updates `parent[x]` to the root node as it unwinds.
Write `union(x, y)` to attach the shorter tree to the root of the taller tree based on the `rank` array.
Use the class to detect a cycle in a set of undirected edges (e.g., 1-2, 2-3, 3-1).

goal: Weekly Graph Traversal Challenge (BFS vs DFS)
description: Practice choosing between Breadth-First Search (BFS) and Depth-First Search (DFS) for grid-based problems.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Sunday afternoon sessions.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must define WHY the specific traversal was chosen (e.g., BFS for shortest path).

tasks.description: Read a grid problem (e.g., Number of Islands, Rotting Oranges) and decide if it needs BFS (shortest path/level order) or DFS (connectivity).
Write the boilerplate code for the chosen method (Queue for BFS, Stack/Recursion for DFS) and the `visited` set.
Implement the 'directions' array (up, down, left, right) loop to explore adjacent cells.

goal: Build an In-Memory File System with a N-ary Tree
description: Create a hierarchical file system (directories and files) using an N-ary tree structure to manage parent-child relationships.

tasks.description: Create a node class containing a name, a boolean 'is_directory', and a map of children.

Create a recursive or iterative function that traverses the tree and adds nodes for missing directories in a path.

goal: Daily Sliding Window Pattern Practice
description: Master the Sliding Window technique to solve subarray and substring problems in O(N) time complexity.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the evening study block (25 mins).

tasks.description: Select a problem tagged 'Sliding Window' on an algorithmic platform.

goal: Implement Segment Tree for Range Sum Queries
description: Construct a Segment Tree to perform range sums and point updates in O(log N) time.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Java or C++.

tasks.description: Implement the `build` function that divides an array into halves and stores the sum in the current node.

Write a function to update a single element and propagate the change up the tree to the root.

goal: Weekly Recursion to Iteration Refactoring Challenge
description: Practice converting recursive algorithms to iterative ones using a manual stack to avoid stack overflow risks and improve memory efficiency.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Sunday morning sessions.
baseline.physical_constraints: Mental effort required to track state without the implicit call stack.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a stack data structure for the iterative version.

tasks.description: Select a recursive algorithm (e.g., In-order Binary Tree Traversal or Factorial) and identify the base and recursive cases.

Rewrite the algorithm using a `while` loop and an explicit `stack` (list/deque).

goal: Implement Prim's Algorithm for Minimum Spanning Tree (MST)
description: Build a Minimum Spanning Tree from a connected, weighted graph to find the minimum cost to connect all nodes.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python with a priority queue.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must start from an arbitrary node and use a greedy approach.

tasks.description: Create an adjacency list for a weighted graph and a set to track nodes already in the MST.
Initialize a min-priority queue with a starting edge (weight 0, start_node).
While nodes are missing from MST, pop the smallest edge, and if the target node is unvisited, add it and push its edges.

goal: Master the Bellman-Ford Algorithm for Shortest Paths
description: Implement the Bellman-Ford algorithm to find the shortest path from a source node to all other nodes in a graph that may contain negative edge weights.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python or C++.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must correctly detect and report negative weight cycles.

tasks.description: Represent the graph as a simple list of tuples (u, v, w) to facilitate the relaxation of all edges in each iteration.
Create an array of distances initialized to infinity, setting the source node distance to 0.
Run a loop V-1 times, relaxing every edge (u, v) in the edge list by updating dist[v] if dist[u] + w < dist[v].
Perform one final iteration over all edges; if any distance can still be reduced, flag a negative cycle.

goal: Daily Monotonic Stack Practice
description: Internalize the Monotonic Stack pattern to solve 'Next Greater Element' or 'Histogram' problems in linear time.

baseline.fixed_constraints: 15-minute practice before the workday starts.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a stack to maintain either strictly increasing or decreasing elements.

tasks.description: Draw an array of 6 numbers and manually trace how a stack would pop elements that are smaller than the current 'new' element.

goal: Implement a Suffix Array for String Pattern Matching

tasks.description: Write a function to create a list of all suffixes for a given string (e.g., 'banana' -> ['banana', 'anana', 'nana', ...]).
Sort the list of suffixes and store their original starting indices in the Suffix Array.

tasks.task: Implement Binary Search on Suffix Array
tasks.description: Write a search function that uses binary search on the sorted suffix array to find the occurrence of a pattern.

goal: Weekly Disjoint Set Union (DSU) Refinement
description: Maintain proficiency in the DSU data structure by re-implementing Path Compression and Union by Rank to solve connectivity problems.

baseline.physical_constraints: None.

tasks.description: Code the `find` and `union` methods from scratch in a blank file.
Manually trace a sequence of 5 unions and 5 finds to verify that path compression is flattening the tree.

goal: Implement Floyd-Warshall for All-Pairs Shortest Paths

baseline.non_negotiables: Must use an adjacency matrix to store intermediate results.

tasks.description: Create a V x V matrix where `matrix[i][j]` is the weight of the edge (i, j), or infinity if no edge exists, and 0 if i == j.
Iterate through intermediate node 'k', then source 'i', then destination 'j', updating `dist[i][j]` using the intermediate node.
Add a second matrix 'next' to store the intermediate nodes used to reconstruct the actual path between any two vertices.

goal: Master the Floyd's Cycle-Finding Algorithm (Tortoise and Hare)

tasks.description: Read the proof of why the slow and fast pointers are guaranteed to meet if a cycle exists.
Write a function that returns True if a cycle is detected and False otherwise.
After collision, move one pointer to head and move both at speed 1 to find the start of the cycle.

tasks.description: Draw the first 2 levels of the decision tree for a 4x4 board to visualize the search space.

goal: Build a Custom Disjoint Set Union (DSU) for Kruskal's Algorithm

tasks.description: Implement a sorting routine for the list of edges based on their weights.

goal: Daily Practice of Binary Search on Range (Answer Space)

baseline.fixed_constraints: Morning commute practice (20 mins).

baseline.non_negotiables: Must identify the 'low', 'high', and 'condition' function for every problem.

tasks.description: Identify the minimum and maximum possible values for the answer (e.g., min speed = 1, max speed = max piles).
Write a boolean function `can_complete(mid)` that checks if a value satisfies the problem constraints in linear time.

goal: Master the A* Search Algorithm for Pathfinding

tasks.description: Write a function to calculate the absolute distance between two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2).

Follow the 'Came_From' pointers from the end node back to the start node to build the final coordinate list.

goal: Implement a Self-Balancing AVL Tree

goal: Build an External Merge Sort for Large Datasets

tasks.description: Read the 1GB file in 100MB chunks, sort each chunk in memory, and save it as a sorted 'run' file on disk.

goal: Daily Practice of Dynamic Programming: State Space Reduction
description: Master the technique of reducing the space complexity of DP solutions (e.g., from O(N^2) to O(N)) by identifying necessary states.

goal: Implement a Fenwick Tree (Binary Indexed Tree) for Prefix Sums

goal: Build a Topological Sort Tool for Task Scheduling
description: Implement Kahn's algorithm using an in-degree array and a queue to find a valid linear ordering of tasks with dependencies.

tasks.task: Build Adjacency List and In-Degree Array
tasks.description: Process a list of pairs (u, v) representing 'u must be done before v' to create a graph and a count of incoming edges for each node.
Identify all nodes with an in-degree of 0 and add them to a FIFO queue.
While the queue is not empty, remove a node, add it to the result, and decrement the in-degree of its neighbors. Add neighbors to the queue if their in-degree becomes 0.

goal: Daily Practice of Binary Search Tree (BST) Validation
description: Master the 'Range Bound' technique for validating a BST to avoid the common mistake of only checking immediate children.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during morning commute (15 mins).

goal: Implement a Bloom Filter for Probabilistic Set Membership
description: Create a space-efficient data structure that can tell if an element is 'definitely not in a set' or 'possibly in a set' using multiple hash functions.

Add 100 items, then check 1000 random strings not in the set to calculate the empirical false positive rate.

goal: Weekly Dynamic Programming: Bitmask Deep-Dive
description: Learn to solve NP-hard problems on small constraints (N < 20) by using bitmasks to represent subsets in DP states.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Saturday morning session (1.5 hours).
baseline.physical_constraints: Binary state representation is cognitively heavy.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must solve at least one variation of the Traveling Salesperson Problem (TSP).

tasks.description: Write down how an integer (e.g., 13 which is 1101 in binary) represents having visited cities 0, 2, and 3.
Write the logic to check if a bit is set `(mask >> i) & 1` and to set a bit `mask | (1 << i)`.
Implement the recursive DP with memoization for a 4-city TSP problem using `dp(mask, current_city)`.

goal: Implement Huffman Coding for Data Compression
description: Build a lossless data compression tool by constructing an optimal prefix-free binary tree based on character frequencies.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python with a Min-Heap (heapq).
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires precise tree traversal to generate variable-length codes.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must produce the mapping of characters to binary strings (e.g., 'a' -> 110).

tasks.description: Write a function to count the occurrences of each character in an input string.
Use a Min-Heap to repeatedly merge the two smallest frequency nodes into a parent node until only one root remains.
Traverse the Huffman tree recursively, assigning '0' for left branches and '1' for right branches to create a lookup table.
Encode the original string into a binary string and then successfully decode it back using the Huffman tree.

goal: Master SQL Query Optimization via EXPLAIN ANALYZE
description: Develop the ability to read execution plans and optimize slow queries by analyzing one complex query daily.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Practice on a local PostgreSQL instance with the 'Pagila' sample dataset.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must reduce the query cost by at least 20% or explain why it is already optimal.

tasks.description: Write a query involving at least 2 JOINs and an aggregate function, then run `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` to record the initial execution time.
Identify the most expensive node in the execution plan (e.g., Sequential Scan, Nested Loop) and hypothesize a fix.
Create a specific B-Tree or Hash index on the columns used in the WHERE or JOIN clauses.
Re-run `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` to verify that the optimizer is using the new index and that costs have decreased.

goal: Design a Cassandra Schema for a Chat Application
description: Create a denormalized data model optimized for write-heavy chat workloads, focusing on Partition Keys and Clustering Columns.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use a free tier of Datastax Astra or local Cassandra container.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires shifting mindset from 'Relational' to 'Query-First' design.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must support the query: 'Get all messages for Room X ordered by time'.

tasks.description: List the top 3 queries the application needs to support (e.g., 'View Room', 'User Login', 'Search Message').
Design the `messages` table, selecting `room_id` as the Partition Key and `timestamp` as the Clustering Column.
Design a second table `messages_by_user` to support the 'Get messages by specific user' query, accepting data duplication.
Insert 1000 sample messages and run the main 'View Room' query to verify sorting order.

goal: Implement Application-Level Database Sharding
description: Build a Python wrapper that routes user data to different SQLite database files based on a hash of the User ID.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python `sqlite3`.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a deterministic hash function (e.g., `UserID % 2`).

tasks.description: Create two empty SQLite databases: `shard_0.db` and `shard_1.db` with identical `users` tables.
Write a function `get_db_connection(user_id)` that returns a connection to shard 0 or 1 based on modulo arithmetic.
Create a script to insert 50 random users using the router function.
Write a function `get_all_users()` that queries both shards and combines the results into a single list.

goal: Demonstrate Database Transaction Isolation Anomalies
description: Write scripts to intentionally reproduce 'Dirty Reads' and 'Non-Repeatable Reads' to understand ACID compliance.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use MySQL or PostgreSQL (SQLite has limited isolation support).
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires running two simultaneous terminal sessions.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must explicitly set the transaction isolation level to 'READ UNCOMMITTED' for the Dirty Read test.

tasks.description: Create a `accounts` table with a single row: `balance = 100`.
Open Transaction A and update balance to 200 (no commit). Open Transaction B (Read Uncommitted) and read the balance.
In Transaction A (Read Committed), read balance. In B, update balance to 300 and commit. In A, read balance again.
Repeat Task 3 but set Transaction A's isolation level to 'SERIALIZABLE'.

goal: Refactor a Spreadsheet into a Normalized Database (3NF)
description: Convert a flat, messy Excel sheet of sales data into a Third Normal Form (3NF) SQL schema to eliminate redundancy.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Input is a single CSV file with mixed customer, product, and order data.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Final schema must have separate tables for Customers, Products, and Orders.

tasks.description: Review the CSV for any cells containing multiple values (e.g., 'Product A, Product B') and plan to split them into atomic rows.
Identify columns that depend only on part of the primary key and move them to new tables (e.g., move Customer Name out of the Orders table).
Remove transitive dependencies (e.g., if 'Zip Code' determines 'City', move Zip/City to a separate location lookup table).
Write a script to read the clean CSV and insert data into the new normalized SQL tables using foreign keys.

goal: Build a Real-Time Distributed Key-Value Store
description: Develop a simplified version of a NoSQL database that supports concurrent reads/writes and basic networking using Go and TCP sockets.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must run on a local network. Using Go 1.21+.
baseline.physical_constraints: High cognitive load when debugging race conditions; sessions limited to 90 minutes.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be thread-safe. Must support 'SET', 'GET', and 'DEL' commands.

tasks.description: Create a thread-safe wrapper around a Go map using sync.RWMutex to store string keys and values.
Write a parser for a subset of the REdis Serialization Protocol (RESP) to handle incoming command strings.
Implement a network listener that accepts multiple concurrent TCP connections and spawns a goroutine for each.
Link the TCP listener and the parser to the storage engine to perform real data operations.
Write a background worker that appends every write command to a 'log.aof' file for crash recovery.

goal: Master Database Indexing with B-Trees and Hash Indexes
description: Daily study and implementation of various indexing strategies to understand how they speed up data retrieval and impact write performance.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the final hour of the workday.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires visualizing tree balancing; recommended to use a whiteboard.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must compare Index Scan vs. Sequential Scan for every test case.

tasks.description: Identify a slow query in a local database and create a B-Tree index on the filtering column.
Draw the insertion of 5 keys into a B-Tree of degree 3, showing the splits and parent promotions.

goal: Build a Document-Store Mock from Scratch
description: Create a Python-based mock of a document database (like MongoDB) that stores JSON-like objects and supports basic filtering.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must use Python 3.x. Data stored as local JSON files.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must support nested field querying (e.g., 'user.profile.name').

tasks.description: Write the logic to write and read dictionaries to individual files named by their unique ID.
Write a function that iterates through all documents and returns those matching a specific key-value pair.
Implement a recursive dot-notation parser to allow querying deep nested objects.

goal: Weekly Database Migration Practice
description: Maintain the ability to safely evolve database schemas without data loss by practicing migrations weekly.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Sunday morning sessions. Using PostgreSQL.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires managing local dev/prod environments.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include a 'Down' script for every migration to revert changes.

tasks.description: Identify a necessary change (e.g., adding a 'not null' constraint or splitting a column) to a test table.
Develop the 'Up' and 'Down' SQL scripts for the change.
Verify that all existing data rows are correctly transformed and no data is truncated.

goal: Configure a High-Availability PostgreSQL Cluster
description: Set up a Primary-Replica architecture to understand streaming replication and failover mechanisms.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use two local Docker containers to represent nodes.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires understanding network bridging between containers.
baseline.non_negotiables: Replica must be in 'Hot Standby' mode, allowing read-only queries.

tasks.description: Enable WAL (Write Ahead Log) archiving and create a replication user with appropriate permissions.
Use the 'pg_basebackup' tool to copy the primary's data directory to the replica's location.
Configure the replica to point to the primary's IP and start the recovery process.
Insert a row into the primary and immediately query the replica to see the data.

goal: Build a Simple Relational Database Engine in Python
description: Create a minimal storage engine that saves data in a CSV-like format but supports SQL-style filtering and table scans.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Must not use any external database libraries. Data must persist in local .txt files.
baseline.physical_constraints: Memory management is handled by Python's garbage collector, but large scans may cause lag.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must support the 'SELECT * FROM table WHERE column = value' operation.

tasks.description: Create a schema definition file that stores column names and data types (e.g., id:int, name:string).
Write a function to convert a Python list into a comma-separated string for disk storage.
Write a function that reads the file line-by-line and yields rows as lists.
Add logic to the table scan to return only rows where a specific index matches a target value.

goal: Daily Review of Database Sharding and Partitioning Strategies
description: Study the differences between horizontal sharding, vertical sharding, and range-based partitioning through daily conceptual drills.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during the morning coffee break (15 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must be able to explain the 'Hotspot' problem for each strategy.

tasks.description: Sketch a diagram of how User IDs 1-1000 would be distributed across 3 shards using Modulo (ID % 3).
Explain out loud the impact of a specific partitioning strategy on cross-shard joins.

goal: Deploy a Redis Cluster for Distributed Caching
description: Configure three Redis instances to act as a cluster with data sharding to understand horizontal scaling.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Docker Compose for local orchestration.
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires at least 2GB of free system RAM.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must verify that keys are automatically assigned to different slots/nodes.

tasks.description: Create 3 redis.conf files, each enabling 'cluster-enabled yes' and specifying a unique port.
Use the 'redis-cli --cluster create' command to link the 3 containers into a single logical unit.
Set 10 different keys and use 'cluster nodes' to see which node stores each key.

goal: Weekly Audit of Slow Query Logs
description: Identify and optimize the worst-performing queries in a personal or staging database environment.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Saturday morning review (30 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: Requires access to the server's file system or administrative DB views.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must address at least one 'Top 5' slow query every session.

tasks.description: Query the `pg_stat_statements` table or read the `slow_query_log` file to find queries with the highest total time.
Run `EXPLAIN` on the slowest query and determine if an index or query rewrite is needed.

goal: Implement a Vector Database Mock for Semantic Search
description: Build a tool that stores text embeddings and performs 'K-Nearest Neighbors' (KNN) search using Cosine Similarity.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python with NumPy.
baseline.physical_constraints: Cosine similarity math is computationally expensive for large lists.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a dot product or cosine distance formula.

tasks.description: Write a function that takes two arrays and returns a score between -1 and 1 representing their similarity.
Create an in-memory dictionary to store IDs as keys and their corresponding 128-dimensional vectors as values.
Write a loop that calculates the similarity between a query vector and every vector in the collection.

goal: Master Recursive Common Table Expressions (CTEs)
description: Learn to use recursive SQL queries to traverse hierarchical data structures like organizational charts or file systems.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use PostgreSQL or SQL Server.
baseline.physical_constraints: Recursion limit errors can crash the session if not careful.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must include a termination condition (WHERE clause) to prevent infinite loops.

tasks.description: Create an `employees` table with `id`, `name`, and `manager_id` columns, inserting a 3-level deep hierarchy.
Draft the initial SELECT statement that retrieves the top-level manager (where manager_id is NULL).
Add the `UNION ALL` and the second SELECT statement that joins the CTE to the `employees` table.
Enhance the query to construct a 'path' string (e.g., 'CEO -> VP -> Manager') for each row.

goal: Build a Recommendation Engine with Neo4j (Graph Database)
description: Model user-product interactions as a graph and write Cypher queries to find 'Products purchased by others who bought X'.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Neo4j Desktop or Sandbox.
baseline.physical_constraints: Graph visualization requires a monitor, not suitable for mobile.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the `MATCH` and `RETURN` clauses effectively.

tasks.description: Sketch the nodes (Person, Product) and relationships (BOUGHT, RATED) on a whiteboard.
Write `CREATE` statements to insert 5 users, 10 products, and 15 purchase relationships.
Write a Cypher query to find products bought by people who also bought 'Product A'.

goal: Perform Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR) in PostgreSQL
description: Simulate a catastrophic data loss (e.g., accidental DROP TABLE) and restore the database to the state exactly before the error occurred.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Requires a local Postgres instance with WAL archiving enabled.
baseline.physical_constraints: High stress scenario; follow steps methodically.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must verify data integrity after the restore.

tasks.description: Configure `postgresql.conf` to set `archive_mode = on` and specify an `archive_command` to copy logs to a backup folder.
Run `pg_basebackup` to create a foundational snapshot of the database.
Insert a timestamped 'marker' row, wait 1 minute, then DROP the table. Note the time of the DROP.
Stop the server, replace the data directory with the base backup, configure `recovery.signal` with the timestamp target, and restart.

goal: Daily CAP Theorem Analysis
description: Build intuition for distributed database trade-offs by analyzing one system daily and classifying it as CP (Consistency/Partition Tolerance) or AP (Availability/Partition Tolerance).

baseline.fixed_constraints: Morning study routine (10 mins).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must identify what happens during a network partition for the chosen system.

tasks.description: Choose a database (e.g., Cassandra, HBase, MongoDB, DynamoDB) to analyze.
Research default behavior: Does it stop accepting writes (CP) or accept writes that might conflict (AP) when nodes lose contact?

goal: Implement a Database Connection Pool from Scratch
description: Create a resource manager that maintains a set of open database connections to reduce the overhead of establishing new TCP handshakes.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Python or Java.
baseline.physical_constraints: Concurrency management is key (locks/semaphores).
baseline.non_negotiables: Must implement `acquire()` and `release()` methods.

tasks.description: Create a class that opens N connections on startup and stores them in a thread-safe queue.
Write the `acquire` method that pops a connection from the queue, blocking if empty until a timeout.
Write the `release` method that returns a connection to the queue and notifies waiting threads.
Add a check (e.g., 'SELECT 1') before handing out a connection to ensure it hasn't timed out.

goal: Implement the Zettelkasten Method for Knowledge Management
description: Set up a digital slip-box (Zettelkasten) using Obsidian to interconnect ideas and improve long-term retention of research.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use Obsidian or Roam Research.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must distinguish between 'Fleeting Notes', 'Literature Notes', and 'Permanent Notes'.

tasks.description: Install Obsidian and create three top-level folders: 'Inbox' (Fleeting), 'References' (Literature), and 'Slipbox' (Permanent).
Read one article and summarize it in a single note within 'References', including the source link/citation.
Identify 3 distinct atomic ideas from the literature note and create 3 separate files in 'Slipbox'.
Use wikilinks (e.g., [[Note Name]]) to connect the new permanent notes to each other or to the index note.

goal: Daily Spaced Repetition System (SRS) Maintenance
description: Commit to clearing the Anki review queue every single day to maximize the benefits of the forgetting curve.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Done during morning transit or coffee.
baseline.physical_constraints: Mental fatigue prevents doing >100 cards/day; adjust settings if overwhelmed.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must answer honestly; do not hit 'Easy' just to clear the queue.

tasks.description: Complete all cards scheduled for today in the main deck.
Identify any card marked as 'Again' more than twice in the session and flag it for rewriting.
Create 5 new cards based on things learned yesterday.

goal: Learn to Touch Type at 80 WPM
description: Retrain muscle memory to type without looking at the keyboard, prioritizing accuracy over speed initially.

baseline.fixed_constraints: 15 minutes of practice before starting work.
baseline.physical_constraints: Ergonomic posture required to prevent RSI.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use the correct finger for every key; no 'hunting and pecking'.

tasks.description: Spend 2 minutes practicing 'asdf jkl;' drills to center the hands.
Complete one lesson on Keybr.com or Monkeytype focusing on current problem keys.
Take one 60-second typing test to measure current WPM.

goal: Master Mind Mapping for Brainstorming
description: Learn and apply the rules of effective mind mapping (radial structure, keywords, colors) to deconstruct a complex topic.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Use blank paper and colored pens (analog preferred for learning).
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use a central image. Must use curved branches.

tasks.description: Draw the main topic (e.g., 'Health') in the center of the landscape page using 3 different colors.
Draw 5-7 thick branches radiating from the center, each representing a major sub-category (e.g., Diet, Sleep, Exercise).
Add thinner branches to each main branch to elaborate on details (e.g., Diet -> Veggies -> Spinach).
Add small icons or sketches next to at least 5 keywords to trigger visual memory.

goal: Weekly Feynman Technique Session
description: Identify gaps in knowledge by attempting to explain a complex concept in simple language.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Friday afternoon review.
baseline.physical_constraints: None.
baseline.non_negotiables: Must use analogies. Must identify where the explanation breaks down.

tasks.description: Choose one topic learned this week that feels 'fuzzy' or difficult.
Write an explanation of the concept as if teaching it to a 12-year-old, avoiding jargon.
Review the explanation and highlight areas where you used complex words to mask a lack of understanding.
Go back to the source material to clarify the specific gap identified in Task 3.

goal: Walk 10,000 steps daily by the end of 90 days
description: Marcus is a 44-year-old night-shift security supervisor who struggles with chronic lower back stiffness and a chaotic sleep schedule. He lives in a neighborhood with limited street lighting, making outdoor walks after his shift feel unsafe. He currently averages only 3,200 steps a day and often feels 'too drained' to move after his 12-hour shifts. The plan must account for his extreme fatigue on workdays and his tendency to oversleep on his days off. A major friction point is his recurring sciatica flare-ups which require him to stop if pain exceeds a dull ache.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Works 6 PM to 6 AM, four days on, three days off. Must be stationary at a desk for 6 hours of the shift.
baseline.physical_constraints: L4-L5 disc protrusion; cannot perform high-impact movements like running or heavy jumping.
baseline.non_negotiables: Sleep must be prioritized between 8 AM and 3 PM; physical therapy stretches must be integrated to prevent injury.

tasks.task: Daily Sciatic Nerve Flossing and Mobility
tasks.description: Perform 10 reps of seated nerve glides and 30 seconds of cat-cow stretches immediately after waking up to manage back stiffness before walking.
During the 15-minute hourly break, walk the perimeter of the interior lobby for 5 minutes rather than sitting in the breakroom. Target: 4,500 total daily steps.
Take a 20-minute continuous walk in a local park during daylight hours to reset circadian rhythm and accumulate steps without the safety concerns of his neighborhood at night.
Increase interior patrol duration by 2 minutes each week. If back pain reaches a 4/10 on the pain scale, revert to the previous week's duration for two days.
Once per week, replace all walking targets with 15 minutes of floor-based core stability exercises (Bird-Dog, Dead Bug) to support the spine. Zero step-count pressure.

goal: Write 500 words of a technical manual draft 4 days per week
description: Elena is a freelance software dev and a single mother of a toddler. She feels most creative at 9 PM but is usually physically exhausted by then. She struggles with 'context switching' between parenting and technical writing. Her main obstacle is the guilt she feels when working while her child is awake, and the 'revenge bedtime procrastination' that leads her to scroll social media instead of writing once the house is quiet.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Childcare from 7 AM to 8 PM; client meetings on Tuesdays and Thursdays at 10 AM.
baseline.physical_constraints: Severe eye strain from 8+ hours of coding; requires blue light management.
baseline.non_negotiables: No screen time 30 minutes before actual sleep; must engage in 15 minutes of direct play with child post-daycare.

tasks.description: Place smartphone in a kitchen drawer at 8:30 PM to eliminate the urge to scroll during the transition to writing time.
While doing the dishes or tidying toys (8:30 PM - 8:45 PM), use a voice memo app to record raw thoughts for the manual to bypass the 'blank page' anxiety.
Sit at the desk with a warm beverage and transcribing/editing the voice memo. Goal is 200 words initially to build the 'sitting down' habit.
Increase word count target to 500 words. If the toddler had a 'bad' night (illness/teething), the goal drops to a 'maintenance' level of 50 words just to keep the streak.

goal: Switch to a Mediterranean-style diet for 90% of weekday meals
description: Sanjay is a 58-year-old accountant with pre-diabetes and a self-described 'addiction' to processed snacks and heavy cream in his tea. He lives in a food desert where the nearest fresh produce market is a 25-minute drive. He finds cooking for one depressing and often defaults to frozen pizza. He needs a plan that doesn't feel restrictive but manages his glucose spikes.

baseline.fixed_constraints: Tax season (Feb-April) requires 60-hour work weeks; limited freezer space.
baseline.physical_constraints: Knee osteoarthritis makes standing for long meal-prep sessions painful.
